title: Justin Bieber

infobox.value: Justin Drew Bieber   (1994-03-01) March 1, 1994 (age 32)  [London, Ontario](/wiki/London,_Ontario "London, Ontario"), Canada

* [Discography](/wiki/Justin_Bieber_discography "Justin Bieber discography") * [videography](/wiki/Justin_Bieber_videography "Justin Bieber videography") * [performances](/wiki/List_of_Justin_Bieber_live_performances "List of Justin Bieber live performances")
[Full list](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Justin_Bieber "List of awards and nominations received by Justin Bieber")

([/ˈbiːbər/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*BEE-bər*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"); born March 1, 1994)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a Canadian singer.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Regarded as a prominent figure in contemporary [popular music](/wiki/Popular_music "Popular music"),[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) he rose to fame in the late 2000s with his debut [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play"), *[My World](/wiki/My_World_(Justin_Bieber_EP) "My World (Justin Bieber EP)")* (2009), receiving international recognition and establishing himself as a [teen idol](/wiki/Teen_idol "Teen idol").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian singer (born 1994)

"Bieber" redirects here. For other uses, see [Bieber (disambiguation)](/wiki/Bieber_(disambiguation) "Bieber (disambiguation)").

Not to be confused with [Justin Beaver](/wiki/Justin_Beaver "Justin Beaver").

**Justin Drew Bieber** ([/ˈbiːbər/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*BEE-bər*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"); born March 1, 1994)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is a Canadian singer.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Regarded as a prominent figure in contemporary [popular music](/wiki/Popular_music "Popular music"),[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) he rose to fame in the late 2000s with his debut [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play"), *[My World](/wiki/My_World_(Justin_Bieber_EP) "My World (Justin Bieber EP)")* (2009), receiving international recognition and establishing himself as a [teen idol](/wiki/Teen_idol "Teen idol").

Bieber's debut album, *[My World 2.0](/wiki/My_World_2.0 "My World 2.0")* (2010), topped the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), making him the youngest solo male to do so in 47 years.[[5]](#cite_note-Billboard2010-5) Its lead single, "[Baby](/wiki/Baby_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Baby (Justin Bieber song)")", became one of the [best selling singles](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_singles_in_the_United_States "List of best-selling singles in the United States") in the United States, while its accompanying music video became [the most disliked video on YouTube](/wiki/List_of_most-disliked_YouTube_videos "List of most-disliked YouTube videos").[[6]](#cite_note-time-20110330-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) His second album, *[Under the Mistletoe](/wiki/Under_the_Mistletoe "Under the Mistletoe")* (2011), became the first [Christmas album](/wiki/Christmas_album "Christmas album") by a male artist to debut atop the chart.[[8]](#cite_note-UMBB200-8) With his third album, *[Believe](/wiki/Believe_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Believe (Justin Bieber album)")* (2012), and [its acoustic re-release](/wiki/Believe_Acoustic "Believe Acoustic"), Bieber became the first artist in *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")'*s history to have five US number-one albums by the age of 18.[[9]](#cite_note-Sinha-Roy-9) Following a series of controversies, he released the [EDM](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music")-infused 2015 single "[Where Are Ü Now](/wiki/Where_Are_%C3%9C_Now "Where Are Ü Now")", marking a departure from his teen idol image.

Bieber's fourth album, *[Purpose](/wiki/Purpose_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Purpose (Justin Bieber album)")* (2015), yielded the US [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") number-ones "[Love Yourself](/wiki/Love_Yourself "Love Yourself")", "[Sorry](/wiki/Sorry_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Sorry (Justin Bieber song)")", and "[What Do You Mean?](/wiki/What_Do_You_Mean%3F "What Do You Mean?")". The songs monopolized the top three of the [UK singles chart](/wiki/UK_singles_chart "UK singles chart"), making Bieber the [first person to do so](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart_records_and_statistics#Acts_to_occupy_the_top_two "UK Singles Chart records and statistics"). In 2017, he featured on the US number-ones "[I'm the One](/wiki/I%27m_the_One_(DJ_Khaled_song) "I'm the One (DJ Khaled song)")" and "[Despacito](/wiki/Despacito_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Despacito (Justin Bieber song)")"; the latter tied the then-record for the [longest-running number-one song in history](/wiki/List_of_Billboard_Hot_100_chart_achievements_and_milestones#Most_weeks_at_number_one "List of Billboard Hot 100 chart achievements and milestones") (16 weeks). With the albums *[Changes](/wiki/Changes_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Changes (Justin Bieber album)")* (2020) and *[Justice](/wiki/Justice_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Justice (Justin Bieber album)")* (2021), Bieber became the youngest solo act to have eight US number-one albums. Also in 2021, he scored his seventh and eighth US number-one singles, "[Peaches](/wiki/Peaches_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Peaches (Justin Bieber song)")" and "[Stay](/wiki/Stay_(The_Kid_Laroi_and_Justin_Bieber_song) "Stay (The Kid Laroi and Justin Bieber song)")". His following albums, *[Swag](/wiki/Swag_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Swag (Justin Bieber album)")* and *[Swag II](/wiki/Swag_II "Swag II")*, were released in 2025.

Bieber is one of the [best-selling music artists](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists") of all time, with over 150 million units sold worldwide and five [diamond](/wiki/Diamond_Certified "Diamond Certified") certifications from the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA).[[10]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-10) [His accolades](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Justin_Bieber "List of awards and nominations received by Justin Bieber") include two [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"), one [Latin Grammy Award](/wiki/Latin_Grammy_Award "Latin Grammy Award"), eight [Juno Awards](/wiki/Juno_Awards "Juno Awards"), two [Brit Awards](/wiki/Brit_Awards "Brit Awards"), 26 [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award "Billboard Music Award"), 18 [American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Awards "American Music Awards"), six [MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Awards "MTV Video Music Awards") and [a record](/wiki/MTV_Europe_Music_Award#Most_nominated_and_winning_artists "MTV Europe Music Award") 22 [MTV Europe Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Europe_Music_Awards "MTV Europe Music Awards"). *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")* named him one of the [100 most influential people in the world](/wiki/Time_100 "Time 100") in 2011, and *[Forbes](/wiki/Forbes "Forbes")* listed him among the top ten [most powerful celebrities](/wiki/Forbes_Celebrity_100 "Forbes Celebrity 100") from 2011 to 2013.[[11]](#cite_note-ForbesC100-11) *Billboard* ranked him the [eighth-greatest pop star of the 21st century](/wiki/Billboard%27s_Greatest_Pop_Stars_of_the_21st_Century "Billboard's Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century").

## Early life

Justin Drew Bieber was born on March 1, 1994, at St. Joseph's Hospital in [London, Ontario](/wiki/London,_Ontario "London, Ontario"),[[12]](#cite_note-12) and was raised in [Stratford](/wiki/Stratford,_Ontario "Stratford, Ontario").[[13]](#cite_note-Parvis-13) His parents Jeremy Jack Bieber and [Pattie Mallette](/wiki/Pattie_Mallette "Pattie Mallette") were both 18 when Bieber was born and split up not long after his birth.[[14]](#cite_note-14) Pattie worked a series of low-paying office jobs, raising Bieber as a single mother in low-income housing. Mallette's mother Diane and stepfather Bruce helped her raise Bieber.[[15]](#cite_note-thestar-15) Bieber has maintained contact with his father.[[16]](#cite_note-nyimage-16) Bieber's ancestry includes [French](/wiki/French_Canadians "French Canadians"), [Irish](/wiki/Irish_Canadians "Irish Canadians"), [English](/wiki/English_Canadians "English Canadians"), [Scottish](/wiki/Scottish_Canadians "Scottish Canadians") and [German](/wiki/German_Canadians "German Canadians").[[17]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEBieber201022,_23-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19)

Bieber has three younger half-siblings; the four have the same father, Jeremy Bieber. Jeremy Bieber and his ex-girlfriend, Erin Wagner,[[20]](#cite_note-20) who broke up in 2014 after seven years together, have two children, daughter Jazmyn and son Jaxon.[[21]](#cite_note-21) Jeremy married his girlfriend Chelsey in February 2018, and they have a daughter named Bay.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23) Bieber also has a stepsister named Allie, the daughter of his stepmother.[[24]](#cite_note-24)
Bieber attended two [French-language immersion](/wiki/French_immersion "French immersion") elementary schools in Stratford, [Jeanne Sauvé Catholic School](/wiki/Jeanne_Sauv%C3%A9 "Jeanne Sauvé")[[15]](#cite_note-thestar-15) and Bedford Public School. In grades 7 and 8, he attended [Stratford Northwestern](/wiki/Stratford_District_Secondary_School "Stratford District Secondary School"). Former teacher, Kim Booker often recalled fond memories of Bieber as her student, and is featured in several interviews. He attended Stratford's [St. Michael Catholic Secondary School](/wiki/St._Michael_Catholic_Secondary_School "St. Michael Catholic Secondary School"), and graduated in 2012[[25]](#cite_note-25) with a 4.0 GPA.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

Growing up, he learned to play the piano, drums, guitar, and trumpet.[[13]](#cite_note-Parvis-13) In early 2007, aged 12, Bieber performed [Ne-Yo](/wiki/Ne-Yo "Ne-Yo")'s "[So Sick](/wiki/So_Sick "So Sick")" for a local singing competition in Stratford, in which he placed second.[[27]](#cite_note-Mitchell2009a-27) Mallette posted a video of the performance on [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") for their family and friends to see. She continued to upload videos of Bieber singing covers of various R&B songs, and Bieber's popularity on the site grew.[[28]](#cite_note-herrera-28) In the same year, Bieber busked shows in front of the [Avon Theatre](/wiki/Stratford_Festival "Stratford Festival") steps with a rented guitar during tourism season.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

## Career

### 2007–2009: Career beginnings and *My World*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/68/Justin_Bieber_2.png/250px-Justin_Bieber_2.png)](/wiki/File:Justin_Bieber_2.png)

Bieber at a signing event in 2009

While searching for videos of a different singer, [Scooter Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun"), a former marketing executive of [So So Def Recordings](/wiki/So_So_Def_Recordings "So So Def Recordings"), clicked on one of Bieber's 2007 [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") videos by accident, where he was [covering](/wiki/Cover_version "Cover version") [Ne-Yo](/wiki/Ne-Yo "Ne-Yo")'s "[So Sick](/wiki/So_Sick "So Sick")".[[16]](#cite_note-nyimage-16)[[30]](#cite_note-30) Impressed, Braun tracked down Bieber's school, the theatre Bieber was performing at, and finally contacted his mother Mallette, who was initially reluctant because of Braun's Judaism. She remembered praying, "God, I gave him to you. You could send me a Christian man, a Christian label!", and, "God, you don't want this Jewish kid to be Justin's man, do you?" However, [church elders](/wiki/Elder_(Christianity) "Elder (Christianity)") convinced her to let Bieber go with Braun. At age 13, Bieber went to [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta"), Georgia, with Braun to record [demo tapes](/wiki/Demo_tapes "Demo tapes").[[16]](#cite_note-nyimage-16) Bieber began singing for [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(singer) "Usher (singer)") one week later. Bieber soon signed with [RBMG](/wiki/RBMG "RBMG") (Raymond Braun Media Group), a joint venture between Braun and Usher.[[31]](#cite_note-coverstory-31) [Justin Timberlake](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake") was reportedly also in the running to sign Bieber but lost the bidding war to Usher, partly due to the idea that two associated singers of the same name would confuse the market.[[31]](#cite_note-coverstory-31)

Usher then sought assistance in finding a label home for the artist from then manager [Chris Hicks](/wiki/Chris_Hicks_(record_executive) "Chris Hicks (record executive)"), who helped engineer an audition with his contact [L.A. Reid](/wiki/L.A._Reid "L.A. Reid") of [the Island Def Jam Music Group](/wiki/The_Island_Def_Jam_Music_Group "The Island Def Jam Music Group"). Reid signed Bieber to [Island Records](/wiki/Island_Records "Island Records") in October 2008 (resulting in a joint venture between RBMG and [Island Records](/wiki/Island_Records "Island Records")) and appointed Hicks as executive vice-president of Def Jam, where he could manage Bieber's career at the label.[[32]](#cite_note-mitchell-32)[[28]](#cite_note-herrera-28)[[31]](#cite_note-coverstory-31) Bieber then moved to Atlanta with his mother to pursue further work with [Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun") and [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(musician) "Usher (musician)").[[31]](#cite_note-coverstory-31) Braun became Bieber's manager in 2008.[[28]](#cite_note-herrera-28)

Bieber's first single, "[One Time](/wiki/One_Time_(Justin_Bieber_song) "One Time (Justin Bieber song)")", was released to radio while Bieber was still recording his debut album.[[33]](#cite_note-Mitchell2009b-33) The song reached number 12 on the [Canadian Hot 100](/wiki/Canadian_Hot_100 "Canadian Hot 100") during its first week of release in July 2009,[[28]](#cite_note-herrera-28) and peaked at number 17 on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") chart in the United States. During fall 2009, it had success in international markets.[[34]](#cite_note-BABYBILL-34) The song was [certified platinum](/wiki/Platinum_record "Platinum record") in Canada and the US and [gold](/wiki/Gold_record "Gold record") in Australia and New Zealand.[[35]](#cite_note-ARIA-35)[[36]](#cite_note-RIANZ-36)[[37]](#cite_note-RIAA-37) His first release, an [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") titled *[My World](/wiki/My_World_(Justin_Bieber_EP) "My World (Justin Bieber EP)")*, was released on November 17, 2009. Critics noted that material from the EP was inspired by singer [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown")'s early work. The album's second single, "[One Less Lonely Girl](/wiki/One_Less_Lonely_Girl "One Less Lonely Girl")", and two [promotional singles](/wiki/Promotional_single "Promotional single"), "[Love Me](/wiki/Love_Me_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Love Me (Justin Bieber song)")" and "[Favorite Girl](/wiki/Favorite_Girl "Favorite Girl")", were released exclusively on the [iTunes Store](/wiki/ITunes_Store "ITunes Store") and charted within the top 40 of the US *Billboard* Hot 100.[[34]](#cite_note-BABYBILL-34) As a result, he became the first solo artist to have four singles chart in the top 40 of the Hot 100 before the release of a debut album.[[38]](#cite_note-4TOP40-38)

"One Less Lonely Girl" was later also released to radio and peaked within the top 20 in Canada and the US, and was certified gold in the latter.[[34]](#cite_note-BABYBILL-34) Following the release of *My World*, Bieber became the first artist to have seven songs from a debut album chart on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[39]](#cite_note-7DEBUT-39) *My World* was eventually certified platinum in the US and double platinum in both Canada and the United Kingdom.[[40]](#cite_note-RIAA2-40)[[41]](#cite_note-PlatinumCanada-41)[[42]](#cite_note-42) To promote the album, Bieber performed on several live shows such as [mtvU](/wiki/MtvU "MtvU")'s VMA 09 Tour, European program *[The Dome](/wiki/The_Dome_(TV_program) "The Dome (TV program)")*, [YTV](/wiki/YTV_(Canadian_TV_channel) "YTV (Canadian TV channel)")'s *[The Next Star](/wiki/The_Next_Star "The Next Star")*, *[The Today Show](/wiki/Today_(American_TV_program) "Today (American TV program)")*, *[The Wendy Williams Show](/wiki/The_Wendy_Williams_Show "The Wendy Williams Show")*, *[Lopez Tonight](/wiki/Lopez_Tonight "Lopez Tonight")*, *[The Ellen DeGeneres Show](/wiki/The_Ellen_DeGeneres_Show "The Ellen DeGeneres Show")*, *[It's On with Alexa Chung](/wiki/It%27s_On_with_Alexa_Chung "It's On with Alexa Chung")*, *[Good Morning America](/wiki/Good_Morning_America "Good Morning America")*, *[Chelsea Lately](/wiki/Chelsea_Lately "Chelsea Lately")*, and [BET](/wiki/BET "BET")'s *[106 & Park](/wiki/106_%26_Park "106 & Park")*. Bieber also guest starred in an episode of *[True Jackson, VP](/wiki/True_Jackson,_VP "True Jackson, VP")* in late 2009.[[43]](#cite_note-43) Bieber performed [Ron Miller](/wiki/Ron_Miller_(songwriter) "Ron Miller (songwriter)") and Bryan Wells's "Someday at Christmas" for US president [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama") and First Lady [Michelle Obama](/wiki/Michelle_Obama "Michelle Obama") at the [White House](/wiki/White_House "White House") for *[Christmas in Washington](/wiki/Christmas_in_Washington "Christmas in Washington")*, which aired on December 20, 2009, on American television broadcaster [TNT](/wiki/TNT_(American_TV_network) "TNT (American TV network)").[[44]](#cite_note-44)

### 2010–2011: *My World 2.0*, *Never Say Never*, and *Under the Mistletoe*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dd/Justin_Bieber_2010_2.jpg/250px-Justin_Bieber_2010_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Bieber_2010_2.jpg)

Bieber performing at the 2010 [White House Easter Egg Roll](/wiki/White_House_Easter_Egg_Roll "White House Easter Egg Roll")

Bieber was a presenter at the [52nd Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/52nd_Annual_Grammy_Awards "52nd Annual Grammy Awards") on January 31, 2010. He was invited to be a vocalist for [the remake](/wiki/We_Are_the_World_25_for_Haiti "We Are the World 25 for Haiti") of the [charity single](/wiki/Charity_record "Charity record") "[We Are the World](/wiki/We_Are_the_World "We Are the World")" for its 25th anniversary to benefit [Haiti](/wiki/Haiti "Haiti") after [the earthquake](/wiki/2010_Haiti_earthquake "2010 Haiti earthquake"). Bieber sings the opening line, which was sung by [Lionel Richie](/wiki/Lionel_Richie "Lionel Richie") in the original version.[[45]](#cite_note-WeAretheworld2-45) On March 12, 2010, a version of [K'naan](/wiki/K%27naan "K'naan")'s "[Wavin' Flag](/wiki/Wavin%27_Flag "Wavin' Flag")", recorded by a collective of Canadian musicians known as [Young Artists for Haiti](/wiki/Young_Artists_for_Haiti "Young Artists for Haiti"), was released. Bieber is featured in the song, performing the closing lines.[[46]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEShapiro2010[httpsbooksgooglecombooksidLyrqE1c66mwCpgPA136_136]-46)

In January 2010, "[Baby](/wiki/Baby_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Baby (Justin Bieber song)")" was released from his debut album, *[My World 2.0](/wiki/My_World_2.0 "My World 2.0")*. The song featured [Ludacris](/wiki/Ludacris "Ludacris"), and became an international hit. It charted at number five on the US *Billboard* Hot 100, peaked at number three on the [Canadian Hot 100](/wiki/Canadian_Hot_100 "Canadian Hot 100")[[47]](#cite_note-47) and reached the top ten in several international markets.[[34]](#cite_note-BABYBILL-34) Promo singles, "[Never Let You Go](/wiki/Never_Let_You_Go_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Never Let You Go (Justin Bieber song)")" and "[U Smile](/wiki/U_Smile "U Smile")", were top 30 hits on the US Hot 100, and top 20 hits in Canada.[[34]](#cite_note-BABYBILL-34) The album has received generally favourable reviews.[[48]](#cite_note-Metacritic-48) It debuted at number one on the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), making Bieber the youngest solo male act to top the chart since [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder") in 1963.[[5]](#cite_note-Billboard2010-5) *My World 2.0* also debuted at number one on the [Canadian Albums Chart](/wiki/Canadian_Albums_Chart "Canadian Albums Chart"), [Irish Albums Chart](/wiki/Irish_Albums_Chart "Irish Albums Chart"), [Australian Albums Chart](/wiki/Australian_Albums_Chart "Australian Albums Chart"), and the [New Zealand Albums Chart](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_New_Zealand "Recording Industry Association of New Zealand")[[34]](#cite_note-BABYBILL-34) and reached the top 10 of fifteen other countries.[[49]](#cite_note-49)[[50]](#cite_note-50)

To promote the album, Bieber appeared on live programs including *[The View](/wiki/The_View_(U.S._TV_series) "The View (U.S. TV series)")*, the 2010 [Kids' Choice Awards](/wiki/Kids%27_Choice_Awards "Kids' Choice Awards"), *[Nightline](/wiki/Nightline "Nightline")*, *[Late Show with David Letterman](/wiki/Late_Show_with_David_Letterman "Late Show with David Letterman")*, *[The Dome](/wiki/The_Dome_(TV_program) "The Dome (TV program)")* and *[106 & Park](/wiki/106_%26_Park "106 & Park")*.[[51]](#cite_note-51) [Sean Kingston](/wiki/Sean_Kingston "Sean Kingston") appeared on the album's next single, "[Eenie Meenie](/wiki/Eenie_Meenie "Eenie Meenie")". The song reached the top ten in the UK and Australia, and the top 20 of most other markets. The following single from *[My World 2.0](/wiki/My_World_2.0 "My World 2.0")*, "[Somebody to Love](/wiki/Somebody_to_Love_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Somebody to Love (Justin Bieber song)")", was released in April 2010, and a [remix](/wiki/Remix "Remix") was released featuring Bieber's mentor [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(singer) "Usher (singer)"). On June 23, Bieber went on his first official headlining tour, the [My World Tour](/wiki/My_World_Tour "My World Tour"), to promote *My World* and *My World 2.0*. In May 2010, Bieber featured in rapper [Soulja Boy](/wiki/Soulja_Boy "Soulja Boy")'s song "Rich Girl".[[52]](#cite_note-52) In July, it was reported that Bieber was the most searched-for celebrity on the Internet.[[53]](#cite_note-53) That same month, his video for "Baby" surpassed [Lady Gaga](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga")'s "[Bad Romance](/wiki/Bad_Romance "Bad Romance")" (2009) as the most viewed, and most disliked, YouTube video at the time.[[54]](#cite_note-54)[[55]](#cite_note-55) In September, it was reported that Bieber accounted for 3% of traffic on [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter"), according to an employee of the social-networking site.[[56]](#cite_note-56)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/75/Justin_Bieber_April_2011_3.jpg/250px-Justin_Bieber_April_2011_3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Bieber_April_2011_3.jpg)

Bieber performing in Indonesia during his [My World Tour](/wiki/My_World_Tour "My World Tour") in 2011

On *My World 2.0*, Bieber's voice was noted to be deeper than it was in his debut [EP](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play"), due to [puberty](/wiki/Vocal_change "Vocal change").[[57]](#cite_note-57) In April 2010, the singer remarked regarding his vocals: "It cracks. Like every teenage boy, I'm dealing with it and I have the best vocal coach in the world ... Some of the notes I hit on "Baby" I can't hit any more. We have to lower the key when I sing live."[[58]](#cite_note-58) Bieber guest-starred in the season premiere of the [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") American crime drama *[CSI: Crime Scene Investigation](/wiki/CSI:_Crime_Scene_Investigation "CSI: Crime Scene Investigation")*, which aired on September 23, 2010. He played a "troubled teen who is faced with a difficult decision regarding his only brother", who is also a serial bomber. Bieber was also in a subsequent episode of the series, which aired on February 17, 2011, in which his character is killed.[[59]](#cite_note-59) Bieber performed a medley of his singles "U Smile", "Baby", and "Somebody to Love", and briefly played the drums, at the [2010 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2010_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2010 MTV Video Music Awards") on September 12, 2010.[[60]](#cite_note-60) Bieber announced in October 2010 that he would be releasing an acoustic album, called *[My Worlds Acoustic](/wiki/My_Worlds_Acoustic "My Worlds Acoustic")*.[[61]](#cite_note-61) It was released on November 26, 2010, in the United States and featured acoustic versions of songs from his previous albums, and accompanied the release of a new song titled "[Pray](/wiki/Pray_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Pray (Justin Bieber song)")". In October 2010, Bieber released his first book, *Justin Bieber: First Step 2 Forever: My Story*, an autobiography with text from Bieber and photographs from [Robert Caplin](/wiki/Robert_Caplin "Robert Caplin").[[62]](#cite_note-62)

A 3-D part-biopic, part-concert film starring Bieber entitled *[Justin Bieber: Never Say Never](/wiki/Justin_Bieber:_Never_Say_Never "Justin Bieber: Never Say Never")*, was released on February 11, 2011, directed by *[Step Up 3D](/wiki/Step_Up_3D "Step Up 3D")* director [Jon M. Chu](/wiki/Jon_Chu "Jon Chu"). It topped the box office with an estimated gross of $12.4 million on its opening day from 3,105 theatres.[[63]](#cite_note-BOM-63) It grossed $30.3 million for the weekend and was narrowly beaten by the romantic comedy *[Just Go with It](/wiki/Just_Go_with_It "Just Go with It")*, which grossed $31 million.[[64]](#cite_note-64) *Never Say Never* reportedly exceeded industry expectations, nearly matching the $31.1 million grossed by [Miley Cyrus](/wiki/Miley_Cyrus "Miley Cyrus")'s 2008 3-D concert film, *[Hannah Montana & Miley Cyrus: Best of Both Worlds Concert](/wiki/Hannah_Montana_%26_Miley_Cyrus:_Best_of_Both_Worlds_Concert "Hannah Montana & Miley Cyrus: Best of Both Worlds Concert")*, which holds the record for the top debut for a music-documentary.[[65]](#cite_note-65) *Never Say Never* grossed a total of $99,034,125 worldwide, becoming the highest-grossing concert or performance film at the global box office.[[66]](#cite_note-66)[[67]](#cite_note-67) The film is accompanied by his second remix album, *[Never Say Never – The Remixes](/wiki/Never_Say_Never_%E2%80%93_The_Remixes "Never Say Never – The Remixes")*, released February 14, 2011, and features remixes of songs from his debut album, with guest appearances from [Miley Cyrus](/wiki/Miley_Cyrus "Miley Cyrus"), [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), and [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), among others.[[68]](#cite_note-68) Bieber was a participating player in the [2011 NBA All-Star Celebrity Game](/wiki/NBA_All-Star_Celebrity_Game#2011_NBA_All-Star_Celebrity_Game "NBA All-Star Celebrity Game"), held on February 18, 2011, and was named the [Most Valuable Player](/wiki/NBA_All-Star_Celebrity_Game#Most_Valuable_Player "NBA All-Star Celebrity Game") (MVP), scoring eight points (3–11 FG) with two rebounds and four assists.[[69]](#cite_note-69) In June 2011, an album track from *Never Say Never* titled "[That Should Be Me](/wiki/That_Should_Be_Me "That Should Be Me")" (featuring [Rascal Flatts](/wiki/Rascal_Flatts "Rascal Flatts")), won him his first award in [country music](/wiki/Country_music "Country music") for Collaborative Video of the Year at the [CMT Music Awards](/wiki/CMT_Music_Awards "CMT Music Awards"). *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")* magazine named Bieber one of the 100 most influential people in the world on [their annual list](/wiki/Time_100 "Time 100").[[70]](#cite_note-70) In June 2011, Bieber was ranked No. 2 on the [Forbes](/wiki/Forbes "Forbes") list of Best-Paid Celebrities under 30. He is the youngest star, and 1 of 7 musicians on the list, having raked in $53 million in a 12-month period.[[71]](#cite_note-71) The same month, his collaborative single "[Next to You](/wiki/Next_to_You_(Chris_Brown_song) "Next to You (Chris Brown song)")" with American singer [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown") was released. The unfinished video for that song was leaked online on June 6, and the official video was released on June 17.

On November 1, 2011, Bieber released the [Christmas-themed](/wiki/Christmas_music "Christmas music") *[Under the Mistletoe](/wiki/Under_the_Mistletoe "Under the Mistletoe")*, his second studio album.[[72]](#cite_note-72) It became the first [Christmas album](/wiki/Christmas_album "Christmas album") by a male artist to debut at No. 1 on the *Billboard* 200, and sold 210,000 copies in its first week of release.[[8]](#cite_note-UMBB200-8) On November 19, 2021, the album was listed among the Greatest of All Time Top Holiday Albums chart by *Billboard*.[[73]](#cite_note-GOAT-THA-73) The first single from the album, "[Mistletoe](/wiki/Mistletoe_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Mistletoe (Justin Bieber song)")", peaked at number one on the US *Billboard* Holiday 100 and Holiday Digital Songs charts.[[74]](#cite_note-74) Bieber released "[All I Want for Christmas Is You (SuperFestive!)](/wiki/All_I_Want_for_Christmas_Is_You#Mariah_Carey_and_Justin_Bieber_version "All I Want for Christmas Is You")" as the second single from the album, which is a re-recorded version of [Mariah Carey](/wiki/Mariah_Carey "Mariah Carey")'s original single, "[All I Want for Christmas Is You](/wiki/All_I_Want_for_Christmas_Is_You "All I Want for Christmas Is You")", with Carey providing vocals on the track.[[75]](#cite_note-75) *Billboard* listed the album and its singles, among the greatest [Holiday](/wiki/Christmas_music "Christmas music") albums and songs of all time, respectively.[[76]](#cite_note-GOAT-H100-76)

### 2012–2014: *Believe*, *Journals*, and other appearances

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/23/Justin_Bieber_NRJ_Music_Awards_2012.jpg/250px-Justin_Bieber_NRJ_Music_Awards_2012.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Bieber_NRJ_Music_Awards_2012.jpg)

Bieber at the 2012 [NRJ Music Awards](/wiki/NRJ_Music_Award "NRJ Music Award") in [Cannes](/wiki/Cannes "Cannes"), France

In late 2011, Bieber began recording his third studio album, titled *[Believe](/wiki/Believe_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Believe (Justin Bieber album)")*.[[77]](#cite_note-77) The following week, Bieber appeared on *[The Ellen DeGeneres Show](/wiki/The_Ellen_DeGeneres_Show "The Ellen DeGeneres Show")* to announce that the first single would be called "[Boyfriend](/wiki/Boyfriend_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Boyfriend (Justin Bieber song)")", and was released on March 26, 2012.[[78]](#cite_note-78) The song debuted at number two on the US [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"), selling a total of 521,000 digital units, the second-highest-ever debut digital sales week.[[79]](#cite_note-debut-79) Bill Werde of *Billboard* noted that it failed to debut at number one because the digital download of the track was available only through the [iTunes Store](/wiki/ITunes_Store "ITunes Store"), "restricting the buying option for those [who] do not frequent the Apple retail store".[[80]](#cite_note-80) "Boyfriend" became Bieber's first single ever to reach the top position on the [Canadian Hot 100](/wiki/Canadian_Hot_100 "Canadian Hot 100") by debuting at number one and staying on for one week.[[79]](#cite_note-debut-79) Bieber was featured on American [hip-hop](/wiki/Hip-hop "Hip-hop") group [Far East Movement](/wiki/Far_East_Movement "Far East Movement")'s song "[Live My Life](/wiki/Live_My_Life_(song) "Live My Life (song)")", from their fourth studio album, *[Dirty Bass](/wiki/Dirty_Bass "Dirty Bass")*, in February 2012. The song emerged online five days before its scheduled release date and peaked within the top 30 of the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[81]](#cite_note-81) The first promotional single from the album, "[Die in Your Arms](/wiki/Die_in_Your_Arms "Die in Your Arms")", was released on May 29, 2012, and the second promotional single, "[All Around the World](/wiki/All_Around_the_World_(Justin_Bieber_song) "All Around the World (Justin Bieber song)")" (featuring American rapper [Ludacris](/wiki/Ludacris "Ludacris")), followed the next week.[[82]](#cite_note-82)[[83]](#cite_note-83) The second single from *Believe*, "[As Long as You Love Me](/wiki/As_Long_as_You_Love_Me_(Justin_Bieber_song) "As Long as You Love Me (Justin Bieber song)")" (featuring [Big Sean](/wiki/Big_Sean "Big Sean")), was released on June 11, 2012. It peaked at number six on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[84]](#cite_note-84)

His third studio album, *Believe*, was released on June 19, 2012, by [Island Records](/wiki/Island_Records "Island Records"). The album marked a musical departure from the [teen pop](/wiki/Teen_pop "Teen pop") sound of his previous releases, and incorporated elements of [dance-pop](/wiki/Dance-pop "Dance-pop") and [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") genres.[[85]](#cite_note-BILLBOARDBIO-85) Intent on developing a more "mature" sound, Bieber collaborated with a wide range of urban producers for the release as well as some long-time collaborators, including [Darkchild](/wiki/Rodney_Jerkins "Rodney Jerkins"), [Hit-Boy](/wiki/Hit-Boy "Hit-Boy"), [Diplo](/wiki/Diplo "Diplo"), and [Max Martin](/wiki/Max_Martin "Max Martin"). *[Entertainment Weekly](/wiki/Entertainment_Weekly "Entertainment Weekly")* praised Bieber's musical shift, calling the album both a "reinvention and a reintroduction".[[86]](#cite_note-ew-86) *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* noted the deeper voice and more "intense" beats found on the album, although it lampooned one of his euphemisms for newfound sexual maturity ("If you spread your wings, you can fly away with me").[[87]](#cite_note-rs-87) *Believe* debuted at number one on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), becoming his fourth number-one album.[[88]](#cite_note-88)[[89]](#cite_note-89)[[90]](#cite_note-bbdebut-90) The album sold 57,000 copies in its first week in Canada, debuting atop the [Canadian Albums Chart](/wiki/Canadian_Albums_Chart "Canadian Albums Chart")[[91]](#cite_note-91). In September 2012, Bieber was featured on "Beautiful", a song from [Carly Rae Jepsen](/wiki/Carly_Rae_Jepsen "Carly Rae Jepsen")'s second studio album, *[Kiss](/wiki/Kiss_(Carly_Rae_Jepsen_album) "Kiss (Carly Rae Jepsen album)")*.[[92]](#cite_note-92) In October 2012, the third single from *Believe*, "[Beauty and a Beat](/wiki/Beauty_and_a_Beat "Beauty and a Beat")" (featuring [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj")), was released. The music video held the record for the most video views in 24 hours when it was released, with 10.6 million views.[[93]](#cite_note-93)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fb/Believe_Tour_12%2C_2012.jpg/250px-Believe_Tour_12%2C_2012.jpg)](/wiki/File:Believe_Tour_12,_2012.jpg)

Bieber performing during his [Believe Tour](/wiki/Believe_Tour "Believe Tour") in October 2012

The [Believe Tour](/wiki/Believe_Tour "Believe Tour"), which further promoted the album, began in September 2012 in [Glendale, Arizona](/wiki/Glendale,_Arizona "Glendale, Arizona").[[94]](#cite_note-94) On December 14, 2012, Bieber appeared on *[The Ellen DeGeneres Show](/wiki/The_Ellen_DeGeneres_Show "The Ellen DeGeneres Show")*, where he announced plans to release an acoustic album titled *[Believe Acoustic](/wiki/Believe_Acoustic "Believe Acoustic")*, which was released on January 29, 2013.[[95]](#cite_note-95) The album debuted at number one on the *Billboard* 200, making Bieber the first artist in history to have five number-one albums in the US before turning 19.[[9]](#cite_note-Sinha-Roy-9)

Bieber returned to *Saturday Night Live* as the host and musical guest on the February 9, 2013, episode. His appearance was panned by critics[[96]](#cite_note-96)[[97]](#cite_note-97) and cast members, including [Kate McKinnon](/wiki/Kate_McKinnon "Kate McKinnon"), who said Bieber was not comfortable with his hosting duties, and [Bill Hader](/wiki/Bill_Hader "Bill Hader"), who said he did not enjoy the presence of Bieber or his entourage. Hader added that in his eight years on the television program, Bieber was the only host who lived up to his reputation.[[98]](#cite_note-98)[[99]](#cite_note-99)

On March 7, 2013, Bieber fainted backstage at London's [O2 Arena](/wiki/The_O2_Arena "The O2 Arena") after complaining of breathing problems throughout his concert performance and was taken to the hospital.[[100]](#cite_note-100) Bieber cancelled his second [Lisbon](/wiki/Lisbon "Lisbon"), Portugal concert at the [Pavilhão Atlântico](/wiki/Pavilh%C3%A3o_Atl%C3%A2ntico "Pavilhão Atlântico"), which was to be held on March 12, because of low ticket sales. The concert held in the same venue on March 11 did go on as scheduled.[[101]](#cite_note-101) In mid-August 2013, a remixed duet version of Michael Jackson's previously unreleased song "[Slave to the Rhythm](/wiki/Slave_to_the_Rhythm_(Michael_Jackson_song) "Slave to the Rhythm (Michael Jackson song)")", featuring Bieber, [leaked](/wiki/Music_leak "Music leak") online.[[102]](#cite_note-102) In response to criticism over this remix, the [Michael Jackson Estate](/wiki/Michael_Jackson_Estate "Michael Jackson Estate") stated that it had not authorized the release of this recording, and has since made attempts to remove the song from as many web sites and [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") channels as possible.[[103]](#cite_note-103) Later, a song titled "Twerk" by rapper [Lil Twist](/wiki/Lil_Twist "Lil Twist"), featuring Bieber as well as [Miley Cyrus](/wiki/Miley_Cyrus "Miley Cyrus"), also leaked.[[104]](#cite_note-104) In September, Bieber was featured in [Maejor Ali](/wiki/Maejor "Maejor")'s song "[Lolly](/wiki/Lolly_(song) "Lolly (song)")" with rapper [Juicy J](/wiki/Juicy_J "Juicy J").[[105]](#cite_note-Weekly,_InTouch-105) A music video for "Melodies", the debut single of American singer [Madison Beer](/wiki/Madison_Beer "Madison Beer"), was released in the same month featuring Bieber in a cameo appearance.[[106]](#cite_note-MTV-106)

On October 3, 2013, Bieber announced that he would release a new song every Monday for 10 weeks as a lead-up to the film *[Justin Bieber's Believe](/wiki/Justin_Bieber%27s_Believe "Justin Bieber's Believe")*, which entered production in May 2012 and was released on December 25, 2013. The film is a follow-up to Bieber's first theatrical film *Justin Bieber: Never Say Never*, with Jon M. Chu returning as director.[[107]](#cite_note-107)[[108]](#cite_note-108)[[109]](#cite_note-109) The first song of Music Mondays, "[Heartbreaker](/wiki/Heartbreaker_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Heartbreaker (Justin Bieber song)")", was released on October 7. The second song, "[All That Matters](/wiki/All_That_Matters_(Justin_Bieber_song) "All That Matters (Justin Bieber song)")", was released on October 14, followed by "[Hold Tight](/wiki/Hold_Tight_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Hold Tight (Justin Bieber song)")" on October 21, "[Recovery](/wiki/Recovery_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Recovery (Justin Bieber song)")" on October 28, "[Bad Day](/wiki/Bad_Day_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Bad Day (Justin Bieber song)")" on November 4, and "[All Bad](/wiki/All_Bad "All Bad")" on November 11. The seventh song, "[PYD](/wiki/PYD_(song) "PYD (song)")" (featuring [R. Kelly](/wiki/R._Kelly "R. Kelly")), was released on November 18; it was followed by "[Roller Coaster](/wiki/Roller_Coaster_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Roller Coaster (Justin Bieber song)")" on November 25, and "[Change Me](/wiki/Change_Me_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Change Me (Justin Bieber song)")" on December 2. The final song, "[Confident](/wiki/Confident_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Confident (Justin Bieber song)")" (featuring [Chance the Rapper](/wiki/Chance_the_Rapper "Chance the Rapper")), was released on December 9, 2013. That same day, it was announced that all 10 tracks would be featured on the compilation album *[Journals](/wiki/Journals_(album) "Journals (album)")*, which would also feature five additional unreleased songs, a music video for "All That Matters", and a trailer for *[Believe](/wiki/Justin_Bieber%27s_Believe "Justin Bieber's Believe")*. *Journals* was only available for purchase via iTunes for a limited time: from December 23, 2013, to January 9, 2014. The titles of the five new additional songs are: "One Life", "Backpack" (featuring [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne")), "What's Hatnin'" (featuring [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)")), "Swap It Out", and "Memphis" (featuring [Big Sean](/wiki/Big_Sean "Big Sean") and [Diplo](/wiki/Diplo "Diplo")).[[110]](#cite_note-110) Bieber released a song titled "[Home to Mama](/wiki/Home_to_Mama "Home to Mama")" (featuring [Cody Simpson](/wiki/Cody_Simpson "Cody Simpson")) in November 2014.[[111]](#cite_note-111) The same month, Bieber topped *[Forbes](/wiki/Forbes_(magazine) "Forbes (magazine)")* magazine's [*Forbes* 30 Under 30](/wiki/Forbes_30_Under_30 "Forbes 30 Under 30") annual ranking, which lists the highest-earning celebrities under 30 for that year.[[112]](#cite_note-112)

Because of the disbanding of [Universal Music](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group")'s division, The Island Def Jam Music Group, in April 2014, Bieber and a number of artists were subsequently transferred to another Universal Music-related division, [Def Jam Recordings](/wiki/Def_Jam_Recordings "Def Jam Recordings"), causing Bieber to no longer be signed to Island Records.[[113]](#cite_note-The_NY_Times-113)[[114]](#cite_note-114)

### 2015–2017: *Purpose*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2f/Justin_Bieber_and_Scooter_Braun_in_Rosemont%2C_Illinois_%282015%29.jpg/250px-Justin_Bieber_and_Scooter_Braun_in_Rosemont%2C_Illinois_%282015%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Bieber_and_Scooter_Braun_in_Rosemont,_Illinois_(2015).jpg)

Bieber and his manager [Scooter Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun") in [Rosemont, Illinois](/wiki/Rosemont,_Illinois "Rosemont, Illinois"), in 2015

In February 2015, Bieber released "[Where Are Ü Now](/wiki/Where_Are_%C3%9C_Now "Where Are Ü Now")", a collaboration with [Jack Ü](/wiki/Jack_%C3%9C "Jack Ü").[[115]](#cite_note-115) The song peaked at number eight on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") and reached number one on *Billboard*'s [Hot Dance/Electronic Songs](/wiki/Hot_Dance/Electronic_Songs "Hot Dance/Electronic Songs") chart.[[116]](#cite_note-116) It earned Bieber his career-first [Grammy Award for Best Dance/Electronic Recording](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Dance/Electronic_Recording "Grammy Award for Best Dance/Electronic Recording") at the [58th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/58th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "58th Annual Grammy Awards").[[117]](#cite_note-117) In March 2015, Bieber made an appearance in the music video for [Carly Rae Jepsen](/wiki/Carly_Rae_Jepsen "Carly Rae Jepsen")'s single "[I Really Like You](/wiki/I_Really_Like_You "I Really Like You")".[[118]](#cite_note-118) In March 2015, Bieber was the featured roastee in [Comedy Central](/wiki/Comedy_Central "Comedy Central")'s annual [roast special](/wiki/Comedy_Central_Roast "Comedy Central Roast"), and was a contestant on the reality competition series *[Lip Sync Battle](/wiki/List_of_Lip_Sync_Battle_episodes "List of Lip Sync Battle episodes")*.[[119]](#cite_note-119) Bieber filmed an episode for the Fox TV reality series *[Knock Knock Live](/wiki/Knock_Knock_Live "Knock Knock Live")*, and aired before the show was cancelled after two episodes.[[120]](#cite_note-120)

On August 28, 2015, Bieber released a new single titled "[What Do You Mean?](/wiki/What_Do_You_Mean%3F "What Do You Mean?")" as the lead single from his fourth studio album, *[Purpose](/wiki/Purpose_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Purpose (Justin Bieber album)")*. The song is a blend of teen pop, [electronic dance music](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music") and acoustic R&B.[[121]](#cite_note-121) It debuted at number one on the US *Billboard* Hot 100 and became Bieber's first number-one single in the country.[[122]](#cite_note-122) He set a [Guinness World Record](/wiki/Guinness_World_Record "Guinness World Record") by becoming the youngest solo male artist to debut at the top of the Hot 100.[[123]](#cite_note-YMSA-123) It also broke the record for the fastest song to reach number one on US iTunes, reaching the top spot in under 5 minutes.[[124]](#cite_note-124) On September 4, 2015, Bieber was co-featured alongside [Young Thug](/wiki/Young_Thug "Young Thug") on the album track "Maria I'm Drunk", from [Travis Scott](/wiki/Travis_Scott "Travis Scott")'s debut studio album, *[Rodeo](/wiki/Rodeo_(Travis_Scott_album) "Rodeo (Travis Scott album)")*. On October 23, 2015, Bieber released the album's second single, titled "[Sorry](/wiki/Sorry_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Sorry (Justin Bieber song)")", which debuted at number two on the *Billboard* Hot 100. After eight non-consecutive weeks at number two, on the week charting January 23, 2016, "Sorry" climbed to the top of the chart and became Bieber's second number-one single on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[125]](#cite_note-125)

The third single from *Purpose*, "[Love Yourself](/wiki/Love_Yourself "Love Yourself")" also peaked at number one in the US, making Bieber the first male artist in almost a decade to have three number-ones from an album since [Justin Timberlake](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake"), who did it previously with his second studio album, *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")*, in 2006–07.[[126]](#cite_note-3NO.1S-126) He also became the first solo artist to chart three solo songs in the top five of the *Billboard* Hot 100 simultaneously, and the first as a lead act since [the Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles") in 1964.[[127]](#cite_note-3TOP5-127) "Love Yourself" topped [*Billboard*'s Year-End Hot 100 chart in 2016](/wiki/Billboard_Year-End_Hot_100_singles_of_2016 "Billboard Year-End Hot 100 singles of 2016"), followed by "Sorry" at number two, and made Bieber only the third artist in history to hold the top-two positions of the [*Billboard* Year-End Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Year-End "Billboard Year-End"), after the Beatles in 1964 and [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(musician) "Usher (musician)") in 2004.[[128]](#cite_note-YEAR-END-128) An album track on *Purpose*, "Company", was announced as the fourth single on March 8, 2016.[[129]](#cite_note-129) On February 12, 2016, Bieber's first four albums were released on vinyl for the first time.[[130]](#cite_note-130)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/Justin_Bieber_20161111_009-2_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Justin_Bieber_20161111_009-2_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Bieber_20161111_009-2_(cropped).jpg)

Bieber performing in [Kraków](/wiki/Krak%C3%B3w "Kraków"), Poland, during the [Purpose World Tour](/wiki/Purpose_World_Tour "Purpose World Tour") in 2016

*Purpose* was released on November 13, 2015, and debuted at number one on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), becoming Bieber's sixth album to debut at the top of that chart.[[131]](#cite_note-131)[[132]](#cite_note-132) It was the fourth best-selling album of 2015 with worldwide sales of 3.1 million copies. As of June 2016, it had sold 4.5 million copies globally.[[133]](#cite_note-wwsales-133) On November 11, 2015, Bieber announced that he would embark on the [Purpose World Tour](/wiki/Purpose_World_Tour "Purpose World Tour"). The worldwide concert tour started in [Seattle](/wiki/Seattle "Seattle"), Washington, on March 9, 2016.[[134]](#cite_note-134) Bieber was accompanied on the tour by his backing band, [We The Band](/wiki/We_The_Band "We The Band").[[135]](#cite_note-135)

On January 8, 2016, Bieber made UK chart history by becoming the [first artist to occupy the entire top three](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart_records_and_statistics#Acts_to_occupy_the_top_two "UK Singles Chart records and statistics") of the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart"). He achieved this feat as "Love Yourself", "Sorry" and "What Do You Mean?" charted at positions 1, 2 and 3 simultaneously.[[136]](#cite_note-UKCHART-136) On May 13, 2016, he was co-featured alongside [Towkio](/wiki/Towkio "Towkio") on the album track "Juke Jam", from [Chance the Rapper](/wiki/Chance_the_Rapper "Chance the Rapper")'s third mixtape *[Coloring Book](/wiki/Coloring_Book_(mixtape) "Coloring Book (mixtape)")*. On July 22, 2016, Bieber released a new single with [EDM](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music") trio [Major Lazer](/wiki/Major_Lazer "Major Lazer") and Danish singer [MØ](/wiki/M%C3%98 "MØ") titled "[Cold Water](/wiki/Cold_Water_(song) "Cold Water (song)")". It debuted at number two on the *Billboard* Hot 100, becoming Bieber's third number-two debut on the ranking, passing [Mariah Carey](/wiki/Mariah_Carey "Mariah Carey")'s record to become the artist with the most number-two debuts in the US at the time.[[137]](#cite_note-Hot_100-137) In August 2016, Bieber was featured on French DJ [DJ Snake](/wiki/DJ_Snake "DJ Snake")'s single "[Let Me Love You](/wiki/Let_Me_Love_You_(DJ_Snake_song) "Let Me Love You (DJ Snake song)")". The song peaked at number four on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[138]](#cite_note-138) Bieber was also featured on American rapper [Post Malone](/wiki/Post_Malone "Post Malone")'s single "[Deja Vu](/wiki/Deja_Vu_(Post_Malone_song) "Deja Vu (Post Malone song)")", which later appeared as the fourth single from the latter's debut studio album, *[Stoney](/wiki/Stoney_(album) "Stoney (album)")*, in September 2016.[[139]](#cite_note-139) Bieber then appeared in the documentary *Bodyguards: Secret Lives from the Watchtower* (2016).[[140]](#cite_note-140) At the [59th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/59th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "59th Annual Grammy Awards"), *Purpose* was nominated for [Album of the Year](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Album_of_the_Year "Grammy Award for Album of the Year") and [Best Pop Vocal Album](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Pop_Vocal_Album "Grammy Award for Best Pop Vocal Album"), whereas "Love Yourself" received nominations for [Song of the Year](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Song_of_the_Year "Grammy Award for Song of the Year") and [Best Pop Solo Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Pop_Solo_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Pop Solo Performance") respectively.

### 2017–2019: Collaborations

On January 28, 2017, Bieber starred in the [2017 NHL Celebrity All-Star Game](/wiki/2017_National_Hockey_League_All-Star_Game#Celebrity_Shootout "2017 National Hockey League All-Star Game") as a participating player, coached by [Wayne Gretzky](/wiki/Wayne_Gretzky "Wayne Gretzky").[[141]](#cite_note-141) On April 17, 2017, Puerto Rican singers [Luis Fonsi](/wiki/Luis_Fonsi "Luis Fonsi") and [Daddy Yankee](/wiki/Daddy_Yankee "Daddy Yankee") released a remix for their song "[Despacito](/wiki/Despacito "Despacito")" featuring Bieber. It was the first song by Bieber in which he sings in [Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language").[[142]](#cite_note-142) The remix became a worldwide success and broke major chart records around the world. The song reached number one in the US and joined "[Macarena](/wiki/Macarena_(song) "Macarena (song)")" (1996) as the only English/Spanish songs to reach number one on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100").[[143]](#cite_note-143) It tied the then-record for the [most weeks at number one](/wiki/List_of_Billboard_Hot_100_chart_achievements_and_milestones#Most_weeks_at_number_one "List of Billboard Hot 100 chart achievements and milestones") in *Billboard* Hot 100 history. The song spent a [record 56 weeks at number one](/wiki/Hot_Latin_Songs#Most_weeks_at_number_one "Hot Latin Songs") on *Billboard*'s [Hot Latin Songs](/wiki/Hot_Latin_Songs "Hot Latin Songs") chart and broke the then-record for [most weeks at number one](/wiki/Digital_Songs#Songs_with_most_weeks_at_number_one_in_sales "Digital Songs") on the [Digital Songs Sales](/wiki/Digital_Songs_Sales "Digital Songs Sales") chart. The remix is the most viewed song of all time on the crowdsourced media knowledge base [Genius](/wiki/Genius_(website) "Genius (website)"), with 23.3 million views.[[144]](#cite_note-144) The song earned Bieber his first career [Latin Grammy](/wiki/Latin_Grammy "Latin Grammy").[[145]](#cite_note-145) As of September 2021, "Despacito" holds the number-one position on the Greatest of All Time Hot Latin Songs chart and number-five on the Greatest of All Time Songs of the Summer chart by *Billboard*.[[146]](#cite_note-GOAT-HLS-146)[[147]](#cite_note-GOAT-SOTS-147)

Bieber, along with rappers [Quavo](/wiki/Quavo "Quavo"), Chance the Rapper, and [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne"), provided vocals on [DJ Khaled](/wiki/DJ_Khaled "DJ Khaled")'s single "[I'm the One](/wiki/I%27m_the_One_(DJ_Khaled_song) "I'm the One (DJ Khaled song)")", released on April 28, 2017. The song debuted at number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100, becoming Bieber's second number-one debut and his fourth song to top the chart.[[148]](#cite_note-I'm_the_One-148) One week later, "Despacito" topped the charts in the US, which became his fifth number-one single and made Bieber the first artist in history to notch new No. 1s in back-to-back weeks.[[149]](#cite_note-NEWNO1S-149) "I'm the One" also reached number one on *Billboard*'s [Hot Rap Songs](/wiki/Hot_Rap_Songs "Hot Rap Songs") and [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") charts.[[150]](#cite_note-150) On June 9, 2017, French DJ [David Guetta](/wiki/David_Guetta "David Guetta") released "[2U](/wiki/2U_(David_Guetta_song) "2U (David Guetta song)")", in which Bieber was featured. The first music video for "2U" features [Victoria's](/wiki/Victoria%27s_Secret "Victoria's Secret") [Secret](/wiki/Victoria%27s_Secret_Swim_Special "Victoria's Secret Swim Special") models lip synching to the song.[[151]](#cite_note-151)[[152]](#cite_note-152)[[153]](#cite_note-153) On July 24, 2017, Bieber cancelled the remaining dates of the [Purpose World Tour](/wiki/Purpose_World_Tour "Purpose World Tour") due to "unforeseen circumstances".[[154]](#cite_note-154)[[155]](#cite_note-155) According to *[Pollstar](/wiki/Pollstar "Pollstar")*, the tour had a total gross of $257 million and 2.8 million in attendance in 162 shows, becoming one of the highest-grossing concert tours of both 2016 and 2017.[[156]](#cite_note-Pollstar_2016_Year_End-156)[[157]](#cite_note-Pollstar_2017_Year_End_Top20-157)

On August 17, 2017, Bieber released the single "[Friends](/wiki/Friends_(Justin_Bieber_and_BloodPop_song) "Friends (Justin Bieber and BloodPop song)")" with American record producer and songwriter [BloodPop](/wiki/BloodPop "BloodPop"). [Julia Michaels](/wiki/Julia_Michaels "Julia Michaels") and [Justin Tranter](/wiki/Justin_Tranter "Justin Tranter") contributed as songwriters, having previously worked with him on "[Sorry](/wiki/Sorry_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Sorry (Justin Bieber song)")" in 2015.[[158]](#cite_note-158) At the [60th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/60th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "60th Annual Grammy Awards"), "Despacito" received three nominations for [Record of the Year](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Record_of_the_Year "Grammy Award for Record of the Year"), [Song of the Year](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Song_of_the_Year "Grammy Award for Song of the Year"), and [Best Pop Duo/Group Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Pop_Duo/Group_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Pop Duo/Group Performance") respectively.[[159]](#cite_note-159) Bieber did not attend the show to perform the nominated song, claiming that he would not make any award show appearances until his next album was finished.[[160]](#cite_note-160) Bieber was a participating player in the [2018 NBA Celebrity All-Star Game](/wiki/NBA_All-Star_Celebrity_Game#2018_NBA_Celebrity_All-Star_Game "NBA All-Star Celebrity Game"), marking his second appearance at the event.[[161]](#cite_note-161) In July 2018, Bieber reunited with DJ Khaled in "[No Brainer](/wiki/No_Brainer_(song) "No Brainer (song)")", co-featuring again with Chance the Rapper and Quavo. The single peaked in the top five of the Hot 100 and reached number one on *Billboard*'s [Hot R&B Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B_Songs "Hot R&B Songs") chart.[[162]](#cite_note-162) He was also featured in the accompanying music video.[[163]](#cite_note-163)

On April 21, 2019, Bieber delivered a surprise performance at the 2019 [Coachella](/wiki/Coachella_Valley_Music_and_Arts_Festival "Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival") music festival, marking his first live performance in two years, and teased his return to music with a new album.[[164]](#cite_note-164) On May 10, 2019, British singer [Ed Sheeran](/wiki/Ed_Sheeran "Ed Sheeran") and Bieber released the single "[I Don't Care](/wiki/I_Don%27t_Care_(Ed_Sheeran_and_Justin_Bieber_song) "I Don't Care (Ed Sheeran and Justin Bieber song)")", from Sheeran's album *[No.6 Collaborations Project](/wiki/No.6_Collaborations_Project "No.6 Collaborations Project")*. The pair had previously collaborated, with Sheeran co-writing Bieber's 2015 song "Love Yourself", and 2016 song "Cold Water" with [Major Lazer](/wiki/Major_Lazer "Major Lazer"). "I Don't Care" became a worldwide hit, reaching number-one in 26 countries, while peaking at number two in the United States.[[165]](#cite_note-EOnline-165) Bieber later featured on a remix of [Billie Eilish](/wiki/Billie_Eilish "Billie Eilish")'s breakthrough single "[Bad Guy](/wiki/Bad_Guy_(Billie_Eilish_song) "Bad Guy (Billie Eilish song)")", which was released on July 11.[[166]](#cite_note-166) On October 4, 2019, Bieber and country music duo [Dan + Shay](/wiki/Dan_%2B_Shay "Dan + Shay") released the song "[10,000 Hours](/wiki/10,000_Hours_(song) "10,000 Hours (song)")", which peaked at number four on the US *Billboard* Hot 100.[[167]](#cite_note-167)[[168]](#cite_note-168) It became the highest-charting non-holiday country song in the history of the *Billboard* [Streaming Songs](/wiki/Streaming_Songs "Streaming Songs") chart and spent 21 weeks at number one on *Billboard*'s [Hot Country Songs](/wiki/Hot_Country_Songs "Hot Country Songs") chart. As a result, Bieber became the first act in history to reach number one on seven multi-metric charts: Hot 100, Hot Country Songs, Hot Dance/Electronic Songs, Hot Latin Songs, Hot R&B Songs, Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs, and Hot Rap Songs.[[169]](#cite_note-MMC-169) It earned Bieber his second [Grammy Award for Best Country Duo/Group Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Country_Duo/Group_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Country Duo/Group Performance") at the [63rd Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/63rd_Annual_Grammy_Awards "63rd Annual Grammy Awards").[[170]](#cite_note-10KGRAMMYS-170) On October 14, 2019, Bieber became the youngest solo male artist to spend 200 cumulative weeks in the top 10 of the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[171]](#cite_note-200WEEKS-171)

### 2020–2022: *Changes* and *Justice*

On December 24, 2019, Bieber announced he would be releasing his fifth studio album and embarking on his fourth concert tour in 2020.[[172]](#cite_note-Complex-172) The album's first single, "[Yummy](/wiki/Yummy_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Yummy (Justin Bieber song)")", was released on January 3, 2020. It debuted at number two on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100").[[173]](#cite_note-billboard1-173) On December 31, 2019, Bieber released a trailer announcing his 10-part [YouTube Originals](/wiki/List_of_YouTube_Premium_original_programming#Docu-series "List of YouTube Premium original programming") [docu-series](/wiki/Television_documentary "Television documentary") *[Justin Bieber: Seasons](/wiki/Justin_Bieber:_Seasons "Justin Bieber: Seasons")*, which focused on an array of themes: his life post-hiatus from music, marriage, preparation for new music, and battle against Lyme disease.[[174]](#cite_note-TheGuardian2019-174) The docu-series amassed 32.6 million views within its first week, breaking the record for the most-viewed premiere in its first week of all YouTube Originals.[[175]](#cite_note-175) Appearing on *The Ellen DeGeneres Show* on January 28, 2020, Bieber confirmed the release date of his fifth studio album, *[Changes](/wiki/Changes_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Changes (Justin Bieber album)")*, to be February 14.[[176]](#cite_note-176) He released a promotional single for the album, "[Get Me](/wiki/Get_Me_(song) "Get Me (song)")" featuring [Kehlani](/wiki/Kehlani "Kehlani").[[177]](#cite_note-177) On February 7, 2020, Bieber released "[Intentions](/wiki/Intentions_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Intentions (Justin Bieber song)")", as the second single from the album. The song peaked at number five on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[178]](#cite_note-178) *Changes* was released on February 14, debuting at number one on the UK and US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") charts, making Bieber the youngest solo artist to have seven number-one albums in the US.[[179]](#cite_note-179) On May 8, 2020, American singer [Ariana Grande](/wiki/Ariana_Grande "Ariana Grande") and Bieber released the single "[Stuck with U](/wiki/Stuck_with_U "Stuck with U")", to help raise funds for the first responders of the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic"). The song debuted atop the *Billboard* Hot 100, becoming his sixth number-one single in the US.[[180]](#cite_note-180) On September 4, 2020, Bieber played a starring role in the music video for [DJ Khaled](/wiki/DJ_Khaled "DJ Khaled")'s single "[Popstar](/wiki/Popstar_(DJ_Khaled_song) "Popstar (DJ Khaled song)")".[[181]](#cite_note-181)

On September 18, 2020, Bieber released "[Holy](/wiki/Holy_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Holy (Justin Bieber song)")" featuring [Chance the Rapper](/wiki/Chance_the_Rapper "Chance the Rapper") as the first single from his upcoming sixth studio album;[[182]](#cite_note-182) it peaked at number three on the *Billboard* Hot 100. On October 15, 2020, he released the album's second single "[Lonely](/wiki/Lonely_(Justin_Bieber_and_Benny_Blanco_song) "Lonely (Justin Bieber and Benny Blanco song)")" in collaboration with [Benny Blanco](/wiki/Benny_Blanco "Benny Blanco"), which peaked at number 12 on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[183]](#cite_note-183) On November 6, 2020, Bieber and [J Balvin](/wiki/J_Balvin "J Balvin") were featured on a remix of [24kGoldn](/wiki/24kGoldn "24kGoldn")'s single "[Mood](/wiki/Mood_(24kGoldn_song) "Mood (24kGoldn song)")".[[184]](#cite_note-184) On November 20, 2020, [Shawn Mendes](/wiki/Shawn_Mendes "Shawn Mendes") and Bieber released "[Monster](/wiki/Monster_(Shawn_Mendes_and_Justin_Bieber_song) "Monster (Shawn Mendes and Justin Bieber song)")";[[185]](#cite_note-185) it peaked at number eight on the *Billboard* Hot 100. On January 1, 2021, Bieber released the third single "[Anyone](/wiki/Anyone_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Anyone (Justin Bieber song)")", which peaked at number six on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[186]](#cite_note-186) On February 14, 2021, Bieber performed "Journals Live" in collaboration with [TikTok](/wiki/TikTok "TikTok"), which marked the first live performance of his 2013 album *[Journals](/wiki/Journals_(album) "Journals (album)")*; it was the first long-form concert event on the platform and broke the record for the most-viewed single-artist livestream in the platform's history.[[187]](#cite_note-187) On February 26, 2021, Bieber announced his sixth studio album would be titled *[Justice](/wiki/Justice_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Justice (Justin Bieber album)")*.[[188]](#cite_note-188) On March 5, 2021, he released the album's fourth single, "[Hold On](/wiki/Hold_On_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Hold On (Justin Bieber song)")", which peaked at number 20 on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[189]](#cite_note-189) On March 19, 2021, Bieber released *Justice* and its fifth single, "[Peaches](/wiki/Peaches_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Peaches (Justin Bieber song)")".[[190]](#cite_note-190)[[191]](#cite_note-191) The album was met with generally positive reviews and received 8 nominations at the [64th Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/64th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "64th Annual Grammy Awards").[[192]](#cite_note-192) It debuted at number one on the *Billboard* 200, becoming Bieber's eighth number-one project, while "Peaches" debuted atop the *Billboard* Hot 100, becoming his seventh number-one single, respectively.[[193]](#cite_note-McIntyre2021-193) As a result, Bieber became the youngest solo artist to attain eight US number-one albums, breaking a 56-year-old record held by [Elvis Presley](/wiki/Elvis_Presley "Elvis Presley"), and the first solo male artist to simultaneously debut a song and an album at number one in the US.[[194]](#cite_note-8NO1ALBUMS-194) He also became the first male act to have his first six studio albums debut at number one on the *Billboard* 200.[[195]](#cite_note-SDEBUT-195)

On [Easter](/wiki/Easter "Easter") 2021, Bieber surprise-released *[Freedom](/wiki/Freedom_(EP) "Freedom (EP)")*, a [gospel](/wiki/Gospel_music "Gospel music")-inspired EP consisting of six songs.[[196]](#cite_note-196) On May 10, 2021, Bieber was featured on the single "[Let It Go](/wiki/Let_It_Go_(DJ_Khaled_song) "Let It Go (DJ Khaled song)")" by DJ Khaled and starred in the accompanying music video.[[197]](#cite_note-197) On July 9, 2021, Bieber released a collaboration with [the Kid Laroi](/wiki/The_Kid_Laroi "The Kid Laroi") titled "[Stay](/wiki/Stay_(The_Kid_Laroi_and_Justin_Bieber_song) "Stay (The Kid Laroi and Justin Bieber song)")". The song reached number one on the *Billboard* Hot 100 in its fourth week, becoming his eighth number-one single in the US.[[198]](#cite_note-8THNO1-198) "Stay" also became his 100th career entry on the chart, making him the then-youngest solo artist to chart 100 songs on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[199]](#cite_note-100ENTRIES-199) "Stay" was the most-streamed song globally on [Apple Music](/wiki/Apple_Music "Apple Music") in 2022.[[200]](#cite_note-200) On July 24, 2021, Bieber headlined the "Freedom Experience" show at the [SoFi Stadium](/wiki/SoFi_Stadium "SoFi Stadium").[[201]](#cite_note-201) On August 13, 2021, Bieber released a remix to Nigerian singer [Wizkid](/wiki/Wizkid "Wizkid")'s song "[Essence](/wiki/Essence_(Wizkid_song) "Essence (Wizkid song)")", which peaked at number nine on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[202]](#cite_note-202)[[203]](#cite_note-203) A week later, he released a collaboration with [Skrillex](/wiki/Skrillex "Skrillex") and [Don Toliver](/wiki/Don_Toliver "Don Toliver") titled "[Don't Go](/wiki/Don%27t_Go_(Skrillex,_Justin_Bieber_and_Don_Toliver_song) "Don't Go (Skrillex, Justin Bieber and Don Toliver song)")".[[204]](#cite_note-204) On September 4, 2021, Bieber headlined the [2021 Made in America Festival](/wiki/Made_in_America_Festival#2021 "Made in America Festival") at the [Benjamin Franklin Parkway](/wiki/Benjamin_Franklin_Parkway "Benjamin Franklin Parkway"). On October 8, 2021, Bieber released the documentary film *[Justin Bieber: Our World](/wiki/Justin_Bieber:_Our_World "Justin Bieber: Our World")*, showcasing his preparation for his 2020 "New Year's Eve Live".[[205]](#cite_note-205) On October 29, 2021, he released "[Rockin' Around the Christmas Tree](/wiki/Rockin%27_Around_the_Christmas_Tree#Justin_Bieber_version "Rockin' Around the Christmas Tree")", a cover of [Brenda Lee](/wiki/Brenda_Lee "Brenda Lee")'s holiday classic.[[206]](#cite_note-206) On November 15, 2021, Bieber announced the international dates of his upcoming fourth concert tour, the [Justice World Tour](/wiki/Justice_World_Tour "Justice World Tour").[[207]](#cite_note-207) On December 5, 2021, Bieber was the headlining performer at the [2021 Saudi Arabian Grand Prix](/wiki/2021_Saudi_Arabian_Grand_Prix "2021 Saudi Arabian Grand Prix").[[208]](#cite_note-208)

On January 1, 2022, Bieber set the then-record for the [most monthly listeners](/wiki/List_of_most-streamed_artists_on_Spotify#Timeline_of_peak_monthly_listeners "List of most-streamed artists on Spotify") on Spotify, with a peak of 94.7 million listeners, later surpassed by [The Weeknd](/wiki/The_Weeknd "The Weeknd").[[209]](#cite_note-209) On March 28, 2022, the sixth single from *Justice*, "[Ghost](/wiki/Ghost_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Ghost (Justin Bieber song)")", peaked at number five on the *Billboard* Hot 100, marking his 20th career top-five hit on the chart.[[210]](#cite_note-210) The single reached number one on *Billboard*'s [Pop Songs](/wiki/Mainstream_Top_40 "Mainstream Top 40") chart, making Bieber the first solo male artist to attain 10 number-one singles in the chart's history.[[211]](#cite_note-GHOST10-211) On March 30, 2022, Bieber was featured on the single "[Up at Night](/wiki/Up_at_Night_(song) "Up at Night (song)")" by Kehlani.[[212]](#cite_note-212) On April 15, 2022, Bieber delivered a surprise performance with Daniel Caesar at the 2022 [Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival](/wiki/Coachella_Valley_Music_and_Arts_Festival "Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival").[[213]](#cite_note-213) On April 29, 2022, Bieber released "[Honest](/wiki/Honest_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Honest (Justin Bieber song)")" featuring Don Toliver and an accompanying music video directed by [Cole Bennett](/wiki/Cole_Bennett "Cole Bennett").[[214]](#cite_note-214) On August 27, 2022, Bieber released "Beautiful Love" as an exclusive single for the battle royale game *[Garena Free Fire](/wiki/Free_Fire_(video_game) "Free Fire (video game)")*.[[215]](#cite_note-215)

### 2023–present: Hiatus, *Swag* and *Swag II*

In January 2023, Bieber sold his music publishing rights and recording catalogue shares, up to the end of 2021, to [Hipgnosis Songs Fund](/wiki/Hipgnosis_Songs_Fund "Hipgnosis Songs Fund")—a sale valued at over $200 million.[[216]](#cite_note-216) Bieber was reportedly offered to be a headlining performer at Coachella 2023, but declined to focus on his seventh studio album.[[217]](#cite_note-217) On February 24, 2023, Don Toliver released the single "[Private Landing](/wiki/Private_Landing "Private Landing")" with Bieber co-featured alongside [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)").[[218]](#cite_note-218) He joined Toliver on stage at the 2023 [Rolling Loud](/wiki/Rolling_Loud "Rolling Loud") festival to deliver a surprise performance of the song.[[219]](#cite_note-219) On September 15, 2023, Bieber released an [acoustic](/wiki/Acoustic_music "Acoustic music") version of the single "[Snooze](/wiki/Snooze_(SZA_song) "Snooze (SZA song)")" by [SZA](/wiki/SZA "SZA") and starred in its music video as the latter's love interest.[[220]](#cite_note-220)

In April 2025, [NetEase Cloud Music](/wiki/NetEase_Cloud_Music "NetEase Cloud Music") named Bieber as the most streamed European/American artist in the platform's 12-year history with more than 11.9 billion streams.[[221]](#cite_note-221) On July 10, 2025, Bieber released his seventh studio album titled [*Swag*](/wiki/Swag_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Swag (Justin Bieber album)") shortly after teasing an upcoming project through billboards in [Iceland](/wiki/Iceland "Iceland"), earlier that same day.[[222]](#cite_note-222) It debuted at number two on the *Billboard* 200 with 163,000 equivalent album units earned in the US in its opening week.[[223]](#cite_note-SWAG-223) The album's lead single "[Daisies](/wiki/Daisies_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Daisies (Justin Bieber song)")" debuted at number two on the *Billboard* Hot 100 and became his 27th top ten single in the US, tying [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson") for the tenth-most in history.[[223]](#cite_note-SWAG-223) On September 4, Bieber announced his album *[Swag II](/wiki/Swag_II "Swag II")* would be released the next day.[[224]](#cite_note-SII-224)

## Artistry

### Influences

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/92/Michael_Jackson_autographing_%27We_Are_The_World%27_posters_1985.jpg/120px-Michael_Jackson_autographing_%27We_Are_The_World%27_posters_1985.jpg)](/wiki/File:Michael_Jackson_autographing_%27We_Are_The_World%27_posters_1985.jpg)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/50/BLAKPROPHETZ-CHRIS_BROWN_-_BET_AWARDS_%28cropped%29.jpg/120px-BLAKPROPHETZ-CHRIS_BROWN_-_BET_AWARDS_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:BLAKPROPHETZ-CHRIS_BROWN_-_BET_AWARDS_(cropped).jpg)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ed/Justin_Timberlake_by_Gage_Skidmore_2.jpg/120px-Justin_Timberlake_by_Gage_Skidmore_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Timberlake_by_Gage_Skidmore_2.jpg)

Bieber cites [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson"), [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), and [Justin Timberlake](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake") as some of his main musical inspirations.

Bieber has cited [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), [Craig David](/wiki/Craig_David "Craig David"), [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson"), [the Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles"), [Boyz II Men](/wiki/Boyz_II_Men "Boyz II Men"), [Justin Timberlake](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake"), [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder"), [Tupac](/wiki/Tupac_Shakur "Tupac Shakur"), [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(musician) "Usher (musician)") and [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West") as his musical inspirations.[[225]](#cite_note-225)[[226]](#cite_note-226)[[227]](#cite_note-227)[[228]](#cite_note-228)[[229]](#cite_note-229)[[230]](#cite_note-230) In 2012, Bieber said, "music is music, and I'm definitely influenced by Michael Jackson and Boyz II Men and people who were black artists—that's what I like."[[231]](#cite_note-231) *Believe* was influenced by Timberlake, for which Bieber was trying to "create a new sound that people aren't really used to hearing ... like when Timberlake did *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")*: It was a new sound... acoustic guitar over hard drums."[[232]](#cite_note-232) In 2019, Bieber called Chris Brown the "best entertainer of all time".[[233]](#cite_note-233)[[234]](#cite_note-234)

### Musical style

Bieber incorporates a variety of genres in his music, focusing mainly on [pop](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music"),[[235]](#cite_note-allmusicgenre-235)[[236]](#cite_note-popcrushgenre-236) and occasionally [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B"),[[236]](#cite_note-popcrushgenre-236)[[237]](#cite_note-237) [dance-pop](/wiki/Dance-pop "Dance-pop") or [EDM](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music").[[235]](#cite_note-allmusicgenre-235) In 2010, [Jody Rosen](/wiki/Jody_Rosen "Jody Rosen") of *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* asserted that the content of his music was "offering a gentle introduction to the mysteries and heartaches of adolescence: songs flushed with romance but notably free of sex itself".[[238]](#cite_note-Rosen-238) During the initial years of his career, his musical style was noted for being aimed to a more [teen pop](/wiki/Teen_pop "Teen pop") and "[bubblegum](/wiki/Bubblegum_pop "Bubblegum pop")-ish" direction.[[239]](#cite_note-Teen-239)

In January 2012, Bieber told *[V](/wiki/V_(American_magazine) "V (American magazine)")* magazine: "I want to do it at my own pace. I don't want to start singing about things like sex, drugs and swearing. I'm into [love](/wiki/Romantic_love "Romantic love"), and maybe I'll get more into making love when I'm older. But I want to be someone who is respected by everybody."[[240]](#cite_note-240) But since then, Bieber gradually altered his artistry, with Peter Gicas of *[E! Online](/wiki/E!_Online "E! Online")* describing "[PYD](/wiki/PYD_(song) "PYD (song)")" as "sexed-up", while *[In Touch Weekly](/wiki/In_Touch_Weekly "In Touch Weekly")* magazine said that his song with [Maejor Ali](/wiki/Maejor "Maejor") and [Juicy J](/wiki/Juicy_J "Juicy J") "[Lolly](/wiki/Lolly_(song) "Lolly (song)")" might make his fans a bit uncomfortable because of its lyrics that refer to [oral sex](/wiki/Oral_sex "Oral sex").[[241]](#cite_note-241)[[105]](#cite_note-Weekly,_InTouch-105) In 2015 Bieber released the [EDM](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music")-fuelled album *[Purpose](/wiki/Purpose_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Purpose (Justin Bieber album)")*, where he collaborated with [Skrillex](/wiki/Skrillex "Skrillex"),[[242]](#cite_note-242) and explored serious themes such as "life experiences", through "feel-good music".[[243]](#cite_note-243)

### Voice

Bieber initially sang with a [boy soprano](/wiki/Boy_soprano "Boy soprano") voice,[[244]](#cite_note-244) before his [voice broke](/wiki/Voice_change "Voice change"), as was evidenced during the debut performance of "[Pray](/wiki/Pray_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Pray (Justin Bieber song)")" at the [2010 American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Awards_of_2010 "American Music Awards of 2010"). [Sean Michaels](/wiki/Sean_Michaels_(writer) "Sean Michaels (writer)") of *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")* described puberty as "the biggest threat to his career" at the time.[[245]](#cite_note-245) [Jody Rosen](/wiki/Jody_Rosen "Jody Rosen") commented that Bieber sings with "swing and rhythmic dexterity" on his debut album, noting his tone to be "nasal".[[238]](#cite_note-Rosen-238) Bieber started to receive voice coaching from [Jan Smith](/wiki/Jan_Smith "Jan Smith") in 2008.[[246]](#cite_note-246)

As an adult singer, Bieber's voice type is [tenor](/wiki/Tenor "Tenor"), with a vocal range spanning from the [baritone](/wiki/Baritone "Baritone") A2 to the high tenor F5.[[247]](#cite_note-247)[[248]](#cite_note-248) In a review of his *[Purpose](/wiki/Purpose_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Purpose (Justin Bieber album)")* album, Neil McCormick of *[The Daily Telegraph](/wiki/The_Daily_Telegraph "The Daily Telegraph")* complimented his "soft, supple and seductive singing".[[249]](#cite_note-249) Caroline Sullivan of *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")* also praised Bieber's "affectedly breathy voice", noting that "the voice soon palls, but the songs are often interesting."[[250]](#cite_note-250) Reviewing his 2021 album, *Justice*, *[Pitchfork](/wiki/Pitchfork_Media "Pitchfork Media")* commented: "Bieber is not a powerhouse vocalist, but he is a compelling one, casually dropping in a stray yodel here, a [Mariah Carey](/wiki/Mariah_Carey "Mariah Carey")–indebted set of runs there. His voice has a palatable smoothness; he's mastered push-and-pull dynamics, and he swings effortlessly from a placid chest voice to a zephyr of a falsetto."[[251]](#cite_note-251)

## Achievements

Main article: [List of awards and nominations received by Justin Bieber](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Justin_Bieber "List of awards and nominations received by Justin Bieber")

Throughout his career, Bieber has sold an estimated 150 million records worldwide, making him one of the [best-selling music artists of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists").[[252]](#cite_note-252)[[253]](#cite_note-Kilkenny-253)

In 2011, Bieber was honoured with a star in front of Avon Theater in [Stratford, Ontario](/wiki/Stratford,_Ontario "Stratford, Ontario"), Canada, where he used to busk when he was younger. On November 23, 2012, Bieber was presented with the [Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal](/wiki/Queen_Elizabeth_II_Diamond_Jubilee_Medal "Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal") by the former Prime Minister of Canada, [Stephen Harper](/wiki/Stephen_Harper "Stephen Harper").
