He was one of 60,000 Canadians to receive the Diamond Jubilee medal that year.[[254]](#cite_note-254) In 2013, Bieber received a [Diamond award](/wiki/Diamond_award "Diamond award") from the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA) for his single "[Baby](/wiki/Baby_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Baby (Justin Bieber song)")", which at the time became the [highest-certified digital single of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_singles_in_the_United_States "List of best-selling singles in the United States").[[255]](#cite_note-255)[[256]](#cite_note-256) Bieber is credited with five [Diamond](/wiki/Diamond_Certified "Diamond Certified") certifications from the RIAA.[[10]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-10) Bieber has won two [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") out of 23 nominations,[[257]](#cite_note-GRAMMY_Awards-257) one [Latin Grammy Award](/wiki/Latin_Grammy_Award "Latin Grammy Award"),[[258]](#cite_note-LATINGRAMMY-258) eight [Juno Awards](/wiki/Juno_Awards "Juno Awards"),[[259]](#cite_note-Juno_Awards-259) two [Brit Awards](/wiki/Brit_Awards "Brit Awards"),[[260]](#cite_note-260)[[261]](#cite_note-261) 26 [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Awards "Billboard Music Awards"),[[262]](#cite_note-262) 18 [American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Awards "American Music Awards"),[[263]](#cite_note-263) 22 [MTV Europe Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Europe_Music_Awards "MTV Europe Music Awards") (the most wins for any artist),[[264]](#cite_note-264) 23 [Teen Choice Awards](/wiki/Teen_Choice_Awards "Teen Choice Awards") (the most wins for a male individual),[[265]](#cite_note-265) eight [iHeartRadio Music Awards](/wiki/IHeartRadio_Music_Awards "IHeartRadio Music Awards"), and six [MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Awards "MTV Video Music Awards"). At age 19, Bieber received the Milestone Award at the [2013 *Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/2013_Billboard_Music_Awards "2013 Billboard Music Awards") in recognition for breaking boundaries with his creativity and contribution to the musical landscape.[[266]](#cite_note-266)[[267]](#cite_note-267) As of 2024, all of Bieber's studio projects are certified [Platinum](/wiki/Music_recording_certification "Music recording certification") or higher by the RIAA and have received numerous accolades.[[268]](#cite_note-268)

Following the release of his fourth studio album, *[Purpose](/wiki/Purpose_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Purpose (Justin Bieber album)")*, Bieber set major milestones globally. He became the first artist, since Elvis Presley in 2005, to replace his own song as number one on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart").[[269]](#cite_note-269) He is the [first artist in history to occupy the entire top three](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart_records_and_statistics#Acts_to_occupy_the_top_two "UK Singles Chart records and statistics") of the UK Singles Chart. He achieved this feat as "[Love Yourself](/wiki/Love_Yourself "Love Yourself")", "Sorry" and "[What Do You Mean?](/wiki/What_Do_You_Mean%3F "What Do You Mean?")" charted at positions 1, 2 and 3 simultaneously.[[136]](#cite_note-UKCHART-136) The singles also peaked at number one in the US, making Bieber the first male artist since [Justin Timberlake](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake") in 2007 to have three number-ones from an album.[[126]](#cite_note-3NO.1S-126) He also became the first solo artist to chart three solo songs in the top five of the US [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") simultaneously, and the first as a lead act since [the Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles") in 1964.[[127]](#cite_note-3TOP5-127) "Love Yourself" topped *Billboard*'s [Year-End Hot 100 Chart in 2016](/wiki/Billboard_Year-End_Hot_100_singles_of_2016 "Billboard Year-End Hot 100 singles of 2016"), followed by "Sorry" at number two, and made Bieber only the third artist in history to hold the top-two positions of the [*Billboard* Year-End Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Year-End "Billboard Year-End"), after the Beatles in 1964 and [Usher](/wiki/Usher_(musician) "Usher (musician)") in 2004.[[128]](#cite_note-YEAR-END-128) As of 2024, Bieber has set 35 *[Guinness World Records](/wiki/Guinness_World_Records "Guinness World Records")* in his career, including eight that were achieved from the success of his album *Purpose*. These records included the most streamed track on Spotify in one week, the most streamed album on Spotify in one week, the most simultaneous tracks on the US *Billboard* Hot 100, and the most simultaneous new entries on the US *Billboard* Hot 100 by a solo artist, among others.[[270]](#cite_note-270)

Bieber has attained success on official charts globally, including the US *Billboard* charts where he has set numerous records: he is the first artist in history to chart new number-one singles in consecutive weeks on the *Billboard* Hot 100;[[149]](#cite_note-NEWNO1S-149) he is the youngest (21) male soloist to debut at number one on the Hot 100;[[123]](#cite_note-YMSA-123) he is the youngest (25) male soloist to spend 200 cumulative weeks in the top 10 of the Hot 100;[[171]](#cite_note-200WEEKS-171) he is the youngest (27) solo artist to have eight number-one albums on the *Billboard* 200, a record held by [Elvis Presley](/wiki/Elvis_Presley "Elvis Presley") since 1965;[[194]](#cite_note-8NO1ALBUMS-194) he is the first male soloist to simultaneously debut a song and an album at number one in the US;[[195]](#cite_note-SDEBUT-195) he is the first male soloist to spend [59 consecutive weeks](/wiki/List_of_Billboard_Hot_100_chart_achievements_and_milestones#Most_consecutive_weeks_in_the_top_10 "List of Billboard Hot 100 chart achievements and milestones") in the top 10 of the Hot 100; he is the first artist to reach number one on seven multi-metric *Billboard* charts: Hot 100, [Hot Country Songs](/wiki/Hot_Country_Songs "Hot Country Songs"), [Hot Dance/Electronic Songs](/wiki/Hot_Dance/Electronic_Songs "Hot Dance/Electronic Songs"), [Hot Latin Songs](/wiki/Hot_Latin_Songs "Hot Latin Songs"), [Hot R&B Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B_Songs "Hot R&B Songs"), [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs"), and [Hot Rap Songs](/wiki/Hot_Rap_Songs "Hot Rap Songs");[[169]](#cite_note-MMC-169) he is the solo artist with the [most cumulative weeks at number one](/wiki/List_of_Billboard_Social_50_number-one_artists "List of Billboard Social 50 number-one artists") (163) on the *Billboard* Social 50 chart. Additionally, he is the artist with the [most number-one debuts](/wiki/Canadian_Hot_100#Number-one_debuts "Canadian Hot 100") (10, tied with [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(musician) "Drake (musician)")), [most number-one singles](/wiki/Canadian_Hot_100#Artists_with_the_most_number-one_hits "Canadian Hot 100") (13, tied with Drake), and the [most cumulative weeks at number one](/wiki/Canadian_Hot_100#Artists_with_the_most_weeks_at_number-one "Canadian Hot 100") (56) on the *Billboard* [Canadian Hot 100](/wiki/Canadian_Hot_100 "Canadian Hot 100"). Bieber's hit single "Despacito" has spent the [most weeks at number one](/wiki/Hot_Latin_Songs#Most_weeks_at_number_one "Hot Latin Songs") (56) on *Billboard*'s Hot Latin Songs chart and is ranked as the magazine's greatest [Latin](/wiki/Latin_music "Latin music") song of all time.[[146]](#cite_note-GOAT-HLS-146) He was named the number one artist on *Billboard*'s Social 50 chart for the 2010s and ranked as the magazine's greatest [pop star](/wiki/Pop_icon "Pop icon") of 2016.[[271]](#cite_note-271)[[272]](#cite_note-272) He was also named *Billboard*'s top male artist for 2016 and ranked seventh on the magazine's Top Artists chart for the 2010s.[[273]](#cite_note-273)[[274]](#cite_note-274) In 2021, *Billboard* ranked him as the 55th greatest artist of all time and the 38th greatest Hot 100 artist of all time, respectively.[[275]](#cite_note-275)[[276]](#cite_note-276)

## Cultural status

### Legacy

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d2/Chairman_of_the_Joint_Chiefs_of_Staff_Adm._Mike_Mullen_Justin_Bieber_The_Daily_Show.jpg/330px-Chairman_of_the_Joint_Chiefs_of_Staff_Adm._Mike_Mullen_Justin_Bieber_The_Daily_Show.jpg)](/wiki/File:Chairman_of_the_Joint_Chiefs_of_Staff_Adm._Mike_Mullen_Justin_Bieber_The_Daily_Show.jpg)

Bieber being greeted by the [US](/wiki/US_Navy "US Navy") [chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff](/wiki/Chairman_of_the_Joint_Chiefs_of_Staff "Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff"), [Mike Mullen](/wiki/Michael_Mullen "Michael Mullen"), in 2011. Bieber is one of Canada's most successful musical exports.[[277]](#cite_note-277)

Bieber has been credited for reinventing pop stardom for over a decade[[278]](#cite_note-278) and has been referred to as the "[Prince of Pop](/wiki/Honorific_nicknames_in_popular_music#B "Honorific nicknames in popular music")"[[279]](#cite_note-279) and the "King of [Teen Pop](/wiki/Teen_Pop "Teen Pop")"[[239]](#cite_note-Teen-239) by contemporary journalists. Highlighting his longevity, he was awarded the [MTV Award for Best New Artist](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Award_for_Best_New_Artist "MTV Video Music Award for Best New Artist") in 2010[[280]](#cite_note-280) and the [MTV Award for Artist of the Year](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Award_for_Artist_of_the_Year "MTV Video Music Award for Artist of the Year") in 2021.[[281]](#cite_note-281) *[Rolling Stone India](/wiki/Rolling_Stone_India "Rolling Stone India")* referred to him as the "biggest popstar of our age", "one of the most captivating artists of the century" and "one of the world's most successful artists of all time".[[282]](#cite_note-282) Bieber is often described as a [pop icon](/wiki/Pop_icon "Pop icon"), or simply an icon.[[283]](#cite_note-283)[[284]](#cite_note-284)[[285]](#cite_note-285)[[286]](#cite_note-286) In a 2011 article comparing the cultural significance of Bieber to [Facebook](/wiki/Facebook "Facebook") founder [Mark Zuckerberg](/wiki/Mark_Zuckerberg "Mark Zuckerberg"), *[Orlando Sentinel](/wiki/Orlando_Sentinel "Orlando Sentinel")* said that, "Out of all the cultural icons who influence and inspire today's society, Justin Bieber is without a doubt one of the most prominent."[[287]](#cite_note-287) He is generally credited as an important figure in bridging [music](/wiki/Music "Music") and [social media](/wiki/Social_media "Social media"), which rose to prominence in the late 2000s; *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* dubbed him "a once-in-a-generation superstar who charted a swift and stunning rise from precocious [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") talent to global phenomenon."[[288]](#cite_note-Ramsay2017-288)

In an article titled "How Justin Bieber revolutionised careers in the music industry" by *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")*, Tom Fazakerley says:

> [Social media](/wiki/Social_media "Social media") has transformed the way people, brands and musicians communicate. The likes of [Myspace](/wiki/Myspace "Myspace") and even more so YouTube, have enabled budding artists to put their music out to a massive audience at the click of a finger. This has taken down the barriers to the music industry and reshaped the career path for budding artists ... Nowadays you can be your own artist, producer and promoter-and if you do this well, like Justin Bieber, you can really make it.[[289]](#cite_note-289)

At age 15, following the release of his 2009 singles "[One Time](/wiki/One_Time_(Justin_Bieber_song) "One Time (Justin Bieber song)")" and "[One Less Lonely Girl](/wiki/One_Less_Lonely_Girl "One Less Lonely Girl")", Bieber's immediate popularity led him to appear on the likes of *[The Ellen DeGeneres Show](/wiki/The_Ellen_DeGeneres_Show "The Ellen DeGeneres Show")*, *[The Wendy Williams Show](/wiki/The_Wendy_Williams_Show "The Wendy Williams Show")* and *[Good Morning America](/wiki/Good_Morning_America "Good Morning America")*.[[290]](#cite_note-Capital2020-290) He had achieved a global fandom who came to be known as Beliebers and his popularity was labelled "Bieber Fever",[[290]](#cite_note-Capital2020-290) with fan frenzies taking place in [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool "Liverpool"),[[291]](#cite_note-291) [Barcelona](/wiki/Barcelona "Barcelona")[[292]](#cite_note-292) and [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"),[[293]](#cite_note-293) among other territories. Bieber's intense fandom was considered to be reminiscent of "[Beatlemania](/wiki/Beatlemania "Beatlemania")",[[294]](#cite_note-294) and his signature [wings](/wiki/Wings_(haircut) "Wings (haircut)") hairstyle at the time also drew comparisons to [the Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles")' [mop top](/wiki/Mop_top "Mop top") hairstyle.[[295]](#cite_note-TheDenverPost2010-295) Bieber's adoration from teenage girls was also likened to those of [Frank Sinatra](/wiki/Frank_Sinatra "Frank Sinatra") in the 1940s and [Elvis Presley](/wiki/Elvis_Presley "Elvis Presley") in the 1950s.[[296]](#cite_note-296)

> He's the only person in humanity who's grown up the way he has – with smartphones and cameras on him 24/7 ... Another kid can go out and have a good night on the town, and no one gives a crap, but Justin is the most [Googled](/wiki/Google "Google") person on the planet – for four years straight!

—[Scooter Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun"), Bieber's manager, in 2013[[297]](#cite_note-willsmith-297)

Bieber maintained his global popularity during his transition to adulthood, achieving increased artistic recognition and credit in the process. In respect to Bieber's success as a teenager to a young adult, *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* said that Bieber is arguably the first mega pop star to [come of age](/wiki/Come_of_age "Come of age") entirely in the social media era and also called him an "Internet icon".[[288]](#cite_note-Ramsay2017-288) *[The Conversation](/wiki/The_Conversation_(website) "The Conversation (website)")*'s Jo Adetunji said that Bieber is "one of the most successful pop singers of recent years".[[298]](#cite_note-Montano2017-298) Regarding his [EDM](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music")-driven album *[Purpose](/wiki/Purpose_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Purpose (Justin Bieber album)")* (2015), which reached "beyond the moment and trends" of that period according to Adetunji, the writer argued that Bieber should be considered a serious creative artist. Adetunji compared the album to [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna")'s *[Ray of Light](/wiki/Ray_of_Light "Ray of Light")* (1998), [Alanis Morissette](/wiki/Alanis_Morissette "Alanis Morissette")'s *[Jagged Little Pill](/wiki/Jagged_Little_Pill "Jagged Little Pill")* (1995) and [Justin Timberlake](/wiki/Justin_Timberlake "Justin Timberlake")'s *[FutureSex/LoveSounds](/wiki/FutureSex/LoveSounds "FutureSex/LoveSounds")* (2006).[[298]](#cite_note-Montano2017-298) In an article about Bieber, Hugh McIntyre of *Forbes* stated, "You may love him, you may hate him, but no matter how you feel about him, nobody can argue that Justin Bieber hasn't conquered the pop world ... The [Grammy](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards") winner can turn essentially any track into a smash, and nobody can sell a single like he can these days."[[299]](#cite_note-McIntyre-299) Also calling Bieber "an unstoppable force in music", McIntyre lauded Bieber for achieving success in various genres, including [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B"), [electronic dance](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music"), [hip hop](/wiki/Hip_hop_music "Hip hop music") and [Latin](/wiki/Latin_music "Latin music").[[299]](#cite_note-McIntyre-299)

Bieber and his work have influenced various recording artists including [Shawn Mendes](/wiki/Shawn_Mendes "Shawn Mendes"),[[300]](#cite_note-300) [Why Don't We](/wiki/Why_Don%27t_We "Why Don't We"),[[301]](#cite_note-301) [Johnny Orlando](/wiki/Johnny_Orlando "Johnny Orlando"),[[302]](#cite_note-302) and [Niall Horan](/wiki/Niall_Horan "Niall Horan").[[303]](#cite_note-303) Singers [Dua Lipa](/wiki/Dua_Lipa "Dua Lipa") and [Charlie Puth](/wiki/Charlie_Puth "Charlie Puth") have stated that they were influenced by the discovery of Bieber on YouTube which inspired them to achieve the same.[[304]](#cite_note-304)[[305]](#cite_note-305)[[306]](#cite_note-306)

### Public image

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b1/Justin_Bieber_Hallenstadion_Zurich_Switzerland.jpg/250px-Justin_Bieber_Hallenstadion_Zurich_Switzerland.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Bieber_Hallenstadion_Zurich_Switzerland.jpg)

Bieber performing in [Zürich](/wiki/Z%C3%BCrich "Zürich") in 2011; he often wore purple clothes at the beginning of his career.

Usher commented that while he and Bieber were both signed at the same age, "I had the chance to ramp up my success, where this has happened to Bieber abruptly". As a result, when he was younger, Usher, Braun, Bieber's bodyguard, and other adults surrounding Bieber constantly coached him on handling fame and his public image.[[16]](#cite_note-nyimage-16) After signing Bieber, Usher appointed one of his former assistants, Ryan Good, to be Bieber's [road manager](/wiki/Road_manager "Road manager") and [stylist](/wiki/Personal_stylist "Personal stylist"). Good, once nicknamed Bieber's "swagger coach", created a "streetwise look" for the singer which consisted of baseball caps, hoodies, dog chains, and flashy sneakers. Amy Kaufman of *[The Los Angeles Times](/wiki/The_Los_Angeles_Times "The Los Angeles Times")* comments, "Though a product of a middle-class suburban upbringing in [Stratford, Ontario](/wiki/Stratford,_Ontario "Stratford, Ontario"), Bieber's manner of dress and speech ('Wassup man, how you doin'?' or 'It's like, you know, whateva' ') suggest he's mimicking his favorite rappers."[[307]](#cite_note-kaufman-307) In 2013, Bieber said he was "very influenced by [black culture](/wiki/African-American_culture "African-American culture")", but he neither thought "of it as black or white" nor tried to "act or pose in a certain way". To him, it was "a lifestyle-like a suaveness or a [swag](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/swag#Etymology_2 "wikt:swag")".[[297]](#cite_note-willsmith-297)

Bieber was often featured in teen magazines such as *[Tiger Beat](/wiki/Tiger_Beat "Tiger Beat")*,[[16]](#cite_note-nyimage-16) and has been labelled a "teen heartthrob".[[308]](#cite_note-308) In 2010, he was frequently criticized for looking and sounding younger than his age,[[309]](#cite_note-Collins-309) and the following year for an [androgynous](/wiki/Androgyny "Androgyny") appearance, which had been commonly noted in the media, including his appearance on the cover of *[LOVE](/wiki/LOVE_(magazine) "LOVE (magazine)")* magazine's androgyny issue in 2011. His signature [wings](/wiki/Wings_(haircut) "Wings (haircut)") hairstyle at the time brought much attention in particular.[[295]](#cite_note-TheDenverPost2010-295) His teen-pop music, image (especially as a heartthrob to girls), and the media attention he receives have additionally been criticized.[[309]](#cite_note-Collins-309) He has been a target of Internet bloggers and [message board](/wiki/Internet_forum "Internet forum") posters, especially users of the Internet message board [4chan](/wiki/4chan "4chan") and of YouTube.[[310]](#cite_note-310) Nick Collins of *[The Daily Telegraph](/wiki/The_Daily_Telegraph "The Daily Telegraph")* said that "Bieber's character appears to strike a particularly sour note with his Internet critics" who have questioned his manner of speech, among other things.[[309]](#cite_note-Collins-309)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c4/Justinbieber2015.jpg/250px-Justinbieber2015.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justinbieber2015.jpg)

Bieber (left) wearing [ripped jeans](/wiki/Ripped_jeans "Ripped jeans") and [skate shoes](/wiki/Skate_shoe "Skate shoe") during his [Purpose World Tour](/wiki/Purpose_World_Tour "Purpose World Tour")

In 2013 and 2014, Bieber's teen heart-throb and clean-cut image was drastically affected due to his involvement in several controversial events. During this period, he abandoned his pet monkey in Germany, vomited onstage, was videoed while urinating in a bucket, cursed at a photograph of [Bill Clinton](/wiki/Bill_Clinton "Bill Clinton"), wore a gas mask in public, allegedly spat on fans (although this was later debunked),[[311]](#cite_note-311) was involved in an explicit image with a stripper, and allegedly assaulted his bodyguard and a limo driver.[[312]](#cite_note-Hoffman2014-312) Bieber's uncle, Brad Bieber, said that his troublesome behaviour was caused by his break-up with [Selena Gomez](/wiki/Selena_Gomez "Selena Gomez").[[312]](#cite_note-Hoffman2014-312) In its March 2014 edition, *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* put Bieber on its cover alongside the title "Bad Boy".[[312]](#cite_note-Hoffman2014-312) The same year, Bieber adopted the [pseudonym](/wiki/Pseudonym "Pseudonym") "Bizzle".[[313]](#cite_note-BizzleInde-313) During the "Bizzle" phase, Bieber donned a [quiff](/wiki/Quiff "Quiff") and often wore thick gold chains and [baseball caps](/wiki/Baseball_cap "Baseball cap").[[312]](#cite_note-Hoffman2014-312)

In his early 20s, following the release of *[Purpose](/wiki/Purpose_(Justin_Bieber_album) "Purpose (Justin Bieber album)")*, Bieber's public image changed in a more positive light. He donned [bleached blond](/wiki/Hair_bleaching "Hair bleaching") hair and often wore [rock](/wiki/Rock_music "Rock music")-branded t-shirts—including [Marilyn Manson](/wiki/Marilyn_Manson "Marilyn Manson") and [Kurt Cobain](/wiki/Kurt_Cobain "Kurt Cobain") t-shirts[[314]](#cite_note-Vogue2016-314)—[flannel shirts](/wiki/Flannel_shirts "Flannel shirts"), [denim jackets](/wiki/Denim_jacket "Denim jacket"), and [ripped jeans](/wiki/Ripped_jeans "Ripped jeans").[[315]](#cite_note-315) He also wore [kilts](/wiki/Kilt "Kilt"), a trend of 1990s fashion, on several occasions.[[314]](#cite_note-Vogue2016-314) *[Vogue](/wiki/Vogue_(magazine) "Vogue (magazine)")* remarked that his fashion during this period was reminiscent of [grunge fashion](/wiki/Grunge_fashion "Grunge fashion") during the 1990s.[[314]](#cite_note-Vogue2016-314) After his marriage in 2018, Bieber's persona continued to change. As his fan base got older and he got married, he transitioned away from the teenage heart-throb image, taking on a more mature personality and a more soft-spoken style of speech.[[316]](#cite_note-316)[[317]](#cite_note-317) In an interview with *[Vogue](/wiki/Vogue_(magazine) "Vogue (magazine)")* in February 2019, Bieber said that he would "laugh at his past self".[[318]](#cite_note-318)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4a/Justin_Bieber_Madame_Tussauds_London.jpg/250px-Justin_Bieber_Madame_Tussauds_London.jpg)](/wiki/File:Justin_Bieber_Madame_Tussauds_London.jpg)

Waxwork of Bieber at [Madame Tussauds](/wiki/Madame_Tussauds "Madame Tussauds"), London

Wax statues of Bieber with the hairstyle from his early career are on display at the [Madame Tussauds](/wiki/Madame_Tussauds "Madame Tussauds") wax museums in New York City, London, and Amsterdam.[[319]](#cite_note-319) In 2018, "Steps to Stardom", an exhibit on Bieber's early career, opened in his hometown of [Stratford, Ontario](/wiki/Stratford,_Ontario "Stratford, Ontario") at the [Stratford Perth Museum](/wiki/List_of_museums_in_Ontario "List of museums in Ontario"), offering a collection of mementos from his formative years and rise to international stardom.[[320]](#cite_note-Friend2018-320) The items on display include a professional drum kit he owned as a younger child, his Grammy Award, microphones, his [Stratford Warriors](/wiki/Stratford_Warriors "Stratford Warriors") hockey jacket, and personal letters, including one from [Michelle Obama](/wiki/Michelle_Obama "Michelle Obama").[[320]](#cite_note-Friend2018-320) Bieber made a number of visits to the museum.[[321]](#cite_note-321)[[322]](#cite_note-322) "Steps to Stardom" was originally scheduled to close in October 2018 but the board of the museum extended its stay for at least another year after the exhibit broke attendance records set by its [Anne Frank House](/wiki/Anne_Frank_House "Anne Frank House") exhibit in 2015.[[323]](#cite_note-323)

At age 17, and within just two years of his professional music career, Bieber was named amongst the [*Time* 100 world's most influential people](/wiki/Time_100 "Time 100") list[[324]](#cite_note-324) and was ranked number 2 on the [*Forbes* Highest-Paid Celebrities Under 30](/wiki/Forbes_30_Under_30 "Forbes 30 Under 30") list.[[325]](#cite_note-Forbes-325) He went on to be included on the *[Forbes](/wiki/Forbes "Forbes")* annual list five more times; in 2012, 2013, 2014, 2016 and 2017.[[325]](#cite_note-Forbes-325) He was also included on *[Forbes](/wiki/Forbes "Forbes")*' list of the top ten [most powerful celebrities](/wiki/Forbes_Celebrity_100 "Forbes Celebrity 100") in 2011, 2012, and 2013.[[11]](#cite_note-ForbesC100-11)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Beliebers_wants_to_see_Justin_Bieber_%287317015488%29.jpg/250px-Beliebers_wants_to_see_Justin_Bieber_%287317015488%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Beliebers_wants_to_see_Justin_Bieber_(7317015488).jpg)

"Beliebers" gathering around the hotel where Bieber is supposed to be inside in [Oslo, Norway](/wiki/Oslo "Oslo") in May 2012

Bieber's early fanbase developed on [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube") and predated the release of his debut album *[My World](/wiki/My_World_(Justin_Bieber_EP) "My World (Justin Bieber EP)")*.[[326]](#cite_note-popularyoutube-326) According to [Jan Hoffman](/wiki/Jan_Hoffman "Jan Hoffman") of *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")*, part of Bieber's appeal stems from his YouTube channel. The *[Chicago Tribune](/wiki/Chicago_Tribune "Chicago Tribune")* noted that Bieber's fanbase, "Beliebers", was among the top words of 2010.[[327]](#cite_note-Chicagtribune-327) Long before the release of *My World* in November 2009, his YouTube videos attracted millions of views.[[16]](#cite_note-nyimage-16) Braun recognized the appeal. Before flying him to Atlanta, Braun wanted to "build him up more on YouTube first" and had Bieber record more home videos for the channel. "I said: 'Justin, sing like there's no one in the room. But let's not use expensive cameras.' We'll give it to kids, let them do the work, so that they feel like it's theirs", recalled Braun.[[16]](#cite_note-nyimage-16) Bieber continues to upload videos to the same channel and has opened a [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") account, from which he interacts with fans regularly.[[28]](#cite_note-herrera-28) In January 2013, Bieber surpassed [Lady Gaga](/wiki/Lady_Gaga "Lady Gaga") as the most-followed person on Twitter for the first time and held the record for 11 months.[[328]](#cite_note-328)

As of 2024, Bieber is the [fourth-most-followed user on Twitter](/wiki/List_of_most-followed_Twitter_accounts "List of most-followed Twitter accounts") and the most-followed musician on the platform, with over 110.3 million followers. His YouTube channel is currently the [most viewed music channel for an individual](/wiki/List_of_most-viewed_YouTube_channels "List of most-viewed YouTube channels") on the platform, having attracted over 32 billion views. With over 73 million subscribers, he remains the most subscribed solo artist on YouTube and held the overall record for six years. 11 [music videos](/wiki/Music_video "Music video") by Bieber have surpassed over 1 billion views on YouTube (second most of all-time),[[329]](#cite_note-329) his most recent being "[Beauty and a Beat](/wiki/Beauty_and_a_Beat "Beauty and a Beat")".[[330]](#cite_note-330) The music video for Bieber's song "[Baby](/wiki/Baby_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Baby (Justin Bieber song)")" is among the [most liked videos](/wiki/List_of_most-liked_YouTube_videos "List of most-liked YouTube videos") on YouTube, having received over 24 million likes since its upload in 2010.[[331]](#cite_note-331) Bieber was frequently a trending topic on Twitter when the feature first launched, as his fans frequently discussed him on the network, and was named the top-trending star on Twitter in 2010.[[332]](#cite_note-biebertwitter-332)[[333]](#cite_note-bieberlink-333)[[334]](#cite_note-toptrender-334)

### Business interests

In 2010, Bieber signed a deal with [Proactiv](/wiki/Proactiv "Proactiv").[[335]](#cite_note-335) In the same year, Bieber partnered with [Nicole by OPI](/wiki/OPI_Products "OPI Products") to launch a nail polish line "The One Less Lonely Girl Collection", which sold exclusively in [Walmart](/wiki/Walmart "Walmart").[[336]](#cite_note-336) Bieber's nail polish line sold one million bottles less than two months after its release.[[337]](#cite_note-337) Bieber endorsed [Adidas](/wiki/Adidas "Adidas"), beside [Derrick Rose](/wiki/Derrick_Rose "Derrick Rose") and [Venus Williams](/wiki/Venus_Williams "Venus Williams"), in 2012.[[338]](#cite_note-338) He became the new "face" and "body" of [Calvin Klein](/wiki/Calvin_Klein_(fashion_house) "Calvin Klein (fashion house)") in early 2015.[[339]](#cite_note-339) [Entertainment Tonight](/wiki/Entertainment_Tonight "Entertainment Tonight") reported that Bieber had used MYO-X, a dietary supplement, in preparation for the photo shoot.[[340]](#cite_note-340) In June 2015, Bieber teamed up with StarShop, a new shopping app launched by [Kevin Harrington](/wiki/Kevin_Harrington_(entrepreneur) "Kevin Harrington (entrepreneur)").[[341]](#cite_note-341) Bieber has been credited with boosting the careers of other singers such as [Carly Rae Jepsen](/wiki/Carly_Rae_Jepsen "Carly Rae Jepsen") and [Madison Beer](/wiki/Madison_Beer "Madison Beer") when he tweeted about them.[[342]](#cite_note-Rolling_Stone-342)[[343]](#cite_note-HuffingtonPost-343)

Bieber has released four fragrances.[[344]](#cite_note-Vena,_Jocelyn-344) He launched his debut fragrance, Someday, in 2011;[[344]](#cite_note-Vena,_Jocelyn-344) it grossed more than three million US dollars in sales, at [Macy's](/wiki/Macy%27s "Macy's"), in just under three weeks, which industry experts regard as a successful celebrity-led launch.[[345]](#cite_note-Elliott2012-345)[[346]](#cite_note-346) On the heels of that 2011 best-seller, he launched his second fragrance, Girlfriend, in June 2012.[[347]](#cite_note-347) His third fragrance, The Key, was launched in July 2013,[[348]](#cite_note-348) and his latest fragrance, Justin Bieber Collector's Edition, launched in 2014.[[345]](#cite_note-Elliott2012-345)

In January 2019, Bieber launched his own clothing line called "Drew House", consisting of a wide range of products. Most products feature the brand's defining symbol of a simple yellow smiley-face logo with the text "drew" written across the front. Bieber trademarked the name for his company in February 2018.[[349]](#cite_note-349) In September 2019, after a year-long collaboration with [Schmidt's Naturals](/wiki/Schmidt%27s_Naturals "Schmidt's Naturals") CEO, Michael Cammarata, Bieber released his deodorant line, "Here + Now", designed for sensitive skin.[[350]](#cite_note-350)[[351]](#cite_note-351)[[352]](#cite_note-352) In October 2020, Bieber collaborated with [Crocs](/wiki/Crocs "Crocs") on a limited edition of the brand's renowned [clogs](/wiki/Clog "Clog") called "Crocs x Justin Bieber". The design draws on Crocs' classic clog range with inputs of purple and yellow, inspired by Bieber's personal clothing brand, Drew House. The limited edition clogs sold at select Crocs and partner e-commerce channels, Drew House's website, and Crocs retail stores in China and South Korea.[[353]](#cite_note-353)

In fall 2021, Bieber collaborated with [Tim Hortons](/wiki/Tim_Hortons "Tim Hortons") to launch a specialty version of [Timbits](/wiki/Timbits "Timbits") known as "timbiebs". [CNN](/wiki/CNN "CNN") credited the promotion's role in contributing to an increase in Tim Hortons' sales by 10.3% in the fourth quarter of 2021.[[354]](#cite_note-354) On February 7, 2022, Bieber starred in [Balenciaga](/wiki/Balenciaga "Balenciaga")'s first 2022 campaign alongside [Kim Kardashian](/wiki/Kim_Kardashian "Kim Kardashian") and [Isabelle Huppert](/wiki/Isabelle_Huppert "Isabelle Huppert").[[355]](#cite_note-355)[[356]](#cite_note-356) In April 2022, Italian brand [Vespa](/wiki/Vespa "Vespa") revealed its collaboration with a new limited edition of its famed scooter curated by Bieber. The "JUSTIN BIEBER X VESPA" is modelled on a Piaggio Sprint base – available in 50, 100 and 100cc guise.[[357]](#cite_note-357) In May 2022, Bieber and [Tim Hortons](/wiki/Tim_Hortons "Tim Hortons") partnered once again in a new collaboration called the "Biebs Brew", a French vanilla cold brew, which was available in North American stores beginning June 6, 2022.[[358]](#cite_note-358)

In December 2022, Bieber launched a clean water technology company called "Generosity" that aims to provide sustainable drinking water by reducing the usage of single-serve plastic. Alongside Micah Cravalho, he showcased 150 water fountains at the [2022 FIFA World Cup](/wiki/2022_FIFA_World_Cup "2022 FIFA World Cup") held in [Qatar](/wiki/Qatar "Qatar"). Generosity fountains dispense refillable alkaline water after connecting to a water source, and are expected to be commercially available at major venues and homes in 2023.[[359]](#cite_note-Generosity-359)

## Personal life

In 2020, it was reported Bieber owned a $26 million home in [Beverly Hills, California](/wiki/Beverly_Hills,_California "Beverly Hills, California"), as well as a lakeside home in [Ontario](/wiki/Ontario "Ontario").[[360]](#cite_note-360)[[361]](#cite_note-361) As Bieber resides in the United States, he is reported to possess a non-immigrant [O-1 visa](/wiki/O_visa "O visa") for working status based on "extraordinary ability or achievement" in an artistic field.[[362]](#cite_note-362) Bieber has said he is not interested in obtaining [U.S. citizenship](/wiki/Citizenship_of_the_United_States "Citizenship of the United States"), and has praised Canada as being "the best country in the world", citing its mostly [government-funded health care system](/wiki/Healthcare_in_Canada "Healthcare in Canada") as a model example.[[363]](#cite_note-RS-363) However, in September 2018, [TMZ](/wiki/TMZ "TMZ") reported that Bieber had begun the process of becoming a United States citizen, following his marriage to [Hailey Baldwin](/wiki/Hailey_Baldwin "Hailey Baldwin").[[364]](#cite_note-364)

### Health

Bieber has struggled with mental health issues,[[365]](#cite_note-365) particularly [depression](/wiki/Depression_(mood) "Depression (mood)") and [anxiety](/wiki/Anxiety "Anxiety"),[[366]](#cite_note-366)[[367]](#cite_note-367) at various points during his career.[[368]](#cite_note-368) He has generally been open about these issues.[[369]](#cite_note-369)[[370]](#cite_note-370) In his American YouTube docu-series *[Seasons](/wiki/Justin_Bieber:_Seasons "Justin Bieber: Seasons")* (2020), the singer opened up about his struggles with addiction, with frequent consumption of the recreational drug [lean](/wiki/Lean_(drug) "Lean (drug)"), pills such as [MDMA](/wiki/MDMA "MDMA"), and [hallucinogenic mushrooms](/wiki/Psilocybin_mushroom "Psilocybin mushroom") in the early stages of his career.[[371]](#cite_note-371)

In January 2020, Bieber announced on his Instagram that he had been diagnosed with [Lyme disease](/wiki/Lyme_disease "Lyme disease"). He also revealed that he had [infectious mononucleosis](/wiki/Infectious_mononucleosis "Infectious mononucleosis"), which affected his neurological and overall health.[[372]](#cite_note-372)

In February 2022, it was reported that Bieber had tested positive for [COVID-19](/wiki/COVID-19 "COVID-19"), two days after embarking on his fourth concert tour, the [Justice World Tour](/wiki/Justice_World_Tour "Justice World Tour").[[373]](#cite_note-373) In June of the same year, Bieber announced that he had been diagnosed with [Ramsay Hunt syndrome type 2](/wiki/Ramsay_Hunt_syndrome_type_2 "Ramsay Hunt syndrome type 2") and that half of his face was [paralyzed](/wiki/Paralysis "Paralysis").[[374]](#cite_note-374) He cancelled and postponed concerts and appearances due to the condition, but returned to live performances in late July.[[375]](#cite_note-375)[[376]](#cite_note-376) In September, two days after the first concert in Latin America as part of the [Rock in Rio](/wiki/Rock_in_Rio "Rock in Rio") festival, it was announced that all remaining dates of the tour would be suspended due to Bieber prioritizing his health. In a statement shared on social media, the singer said that "After getting off stage, the exhaustion overtook me and I realized that I need to make my health the priority right now. So I'm going to take a break from touring for the time being. I'm going to be OK, but I need time to rest and get better."[[377]](#cite_note-Peoplemag-377)

### Relationships

Bieber's father, Jeremy, is a former carpenter and [pro-am](/wiki/Pro-am "Pro-am") mixed martial artist.[[378]](#cite_note-378) In March 2014, *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* characterized Jeremy as having "split with Justin's mom when Justin was a toddler, and wasn't always around afterward. But he has, as of late, accepted a place of honor in his superstar son's entourage".[[379]](#cite_note-379)

From 2008 to 2009, Bieber was in a relationship with Caitlin Beadles; the two remained friends, and Beadles attended Bieber's wedding.[[380]](#cite_note-380) From December 2010 to March 2018, Bieber was in an [on-again, off-again relationship](/wiki/On-again,_off-again_relationship "On-again, off-again relationship") with singer and actress [Selena Gomez](/wiki/Selena_Gomez "Selena Gomez").[[381]](#cite_note-381)[[382]](#cite_note-382)[[383]](#cite_note-383)[[384]](#cite_note-384) In an interview on *[The Ellen DeGeneres Show](/wiki/The_Ellen_DeGeneres_Show "The Ellen DeGeneres Show")*, Bieber stated that some of his songs including "[Sorry](/wiki/Sorry_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Sorry (Justin Bieber song)")", "Mark My Words", and "[What Do You Mean?](/wiki/What_Do_You_Mean%3F "What Do You Mean?")" are about his relationship with Gomez.[[385]](#cite_note-385) From August to December 2016, he was linked to model [Sofia Richie](/wiki/Sofia_Richie "Sofia Richie").[[386]](#cite_note-386)[[387]](#cite_note-387)

Bieber became engaged to model and longtime friend [Hailey Baldwin](/wiki/Hailey_Baldwin "Hailey Baldwin") on July 7, 2018.[[388]](#cite_note-388) They had briefly dated from December 2015 to January 2016, before reconciling in June 2018.[[389]](#cite_note-389) Bieber and Baldwin reportedly obtained a marriage license in September 2018, leading to reports that they had a civil marriage.[[390]](#cite_note-390)[[391]](#cite_note-Maybe-391) On September 14, 2018, Baldwin said on Twitter that she and Bieber had not yet married, but deleted the tweet afterward.[[391]](#cite_note-Maybe-391) On November 23, 2018, Bieber stated he was married to Baldwin.[[392]](#cite_note-Married-392) Bieber and Baldwin had an official ceremony in [Bluffton, South Carolina](/wiki/Bluffton,_South_Carolina "Bluffton, South Carolina"), on September 30, 2019.[[393]](#cite_note-393) On May 9, 2024, Bieber announced through an Instagram post that he and Baldwin were expecting their first child.[[394]](#cite_note-394) On August 23, 2024, they welcomed a boy, Jack Blues Bieber.[[395]](#cite_note-395)[[396]](#cite_note-396)

### Religious beliefs

Bieber has described himself as a faithful [Christian](/wiki/Christian "Christian"), said he communicates with [God](/wiki/God_in_Christianity "God in Christianity") via prayer, and that "He's the reason I'm here".[[397]](#cite_note-guardian-397) He reflected his faith in a music video with Brandon Burke, titled "#iPledge", in which he talks about God's forgiveness.[[398]](#cite_note-Martinez-398) Bieber was baptized on January 9, 2014, by [Pentecostal](/wiki/Pentecostal "Pentecostal") pastor [Carl Lentz](/wiki/Carl_Lentz "Carl Lentz") of [Hillsong Church](/wiki/Hillsong_Church "Hillsong Church"), New York, after a [born again](/wiki/Born_again "Born again") experience. He has described Lentz as a good friend.[[399]](#cite_note-399)[[400]](#cite_note-400) In 2021, he announced that he had become a member of Churchome, an [evangelical](/wiki/Evangelicalism "Evangelicalism") church.[[401]](#cite_note-401) On July 24, 2021, Bieber led worship with [Gospel](/wiki/Gospel_music "Gospel music") singers [Kari Jobe](/wiki/Kari_Jobe "Kari Jobe") and [Cody Carnes](/wiki/Cody_Carnes "Cody Carnes") during the "Freedom Experience" at [SoFi Stadium](/wiki/SoFi_Stadium "SoFi Stadium").[[402]](#cite_note-402) The trio sang ["The Blessing"](/wiki/The_Blessing_(song) "The Blessing (song)").[[403]](#cite_note-403)

When asked how he wants to raise his children, Bieber replied, "I'm a [Jesus](/wiki/Jesus "Jesus") follower. When you accept Jesus, you walk with the [Holy Spirit](/wiki/Holy_Spirit_in_Christianity "Holy Spirit in Christianity"). I just want to be led by the Holy Spirit."[[404]](#cite_note-404)[[405]](#cite_note-405) Many of Bieber's tattoos have religious significance including the medium-sized cross on his chest and a tiny cross under his eye. Bieber also covered up the "Son of God" text tattooed on his abdomen with a large design that features two angels, gothic arches, a skeleton, and a serpent.[[406]](#cite_note-406)

### Opinions

Regarding [sexual abstinence](/wiki/Sexual_abstinence "Sexual abstinence"), Bieber told music magazine *Rolling Stone* in 2011, "I don't think you should have sex with anyone unless you love them." He added that he does not "believe in [abortion](/wiki/Abortion "Abortion")", and that it is "like killing a baby". When asked about the case of abortion with regard to rape, he said, "I guess I haven't been in that position, so I wouldn't be able to judge that."[[363]](#cite_note-RS-363)[[407]](#cite_note-407) In 2022, amidst *[Roe v. Wade](/wiki/Roe_v._Wade "Roe v. Wade")* being overturned, both Bieber and his wife expressed disapproval, with Bieber posting on his [Instagram story](/wiki/Instagram_story "Instagram story") stating "For what it's worth, I think women should have the choice what to do with their own bodies."[[408]](#cite_note-408) His view on sexual orientation is quoted as "everyone's own decision",[[409]](#cite_note-RS2-409) and he has contributed to the [It Gets Better Project](/wiki/It_Gets_Better_Project "It Gets Better Project"),[[410]](#cite_note-410) a non-profit group aiming to prevent [suicide among LGBT youth](/wiki/Suicide_among_LGBT_youth "Suicide among LGBT youth").[[411]](#cite_note-411) Bieber also opposed the [Trump administration family separation policy](/wiki/Trump_administration_family_separation_policy "Trump administration family separation policy"), calling for [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") to "also let those kids out of cages" in 2019.[[412]](#cite_note-412)

In 2011, Bieber was among the list of content creators opposing [Bill S.978](/wiki/Bill_S.978 "Bill S.978"), also known as the [Commercial Felony Streaming Act](/wiki/Commercial_Felony_Streaming_Act "Commercial Felony Streaming Act"),[[413]](#cite_note-klobuchar-413) which would have made unauthorized streaming a felony instead of a misdemeanor.[[414]](#cite_note-414) He stated that the bill's sponsor, U.S. Senator [Amy Klobuchar](/wiki/Amy_Klobuchar "Amy Klobuchar"), "needs to be locked up, put away in cuffs!"[[413]](#cite_note-klobuchar-413) While the bill did not ultimately pass,[[415]](#cite_note-415) it was later reintroduced by Senator [Thom Tillis](/wiki/Thom_Tillis "Thom Tillis") as the [Protecting Lawful Streaming Act](/wiki/Protecting_Lawful_Streaming_Act "Protecting Lawful Streaming Act"),[[416]](#cite_note-416) and was signed into law by then-President [Donald Trump](/wiki/Donald_Trump "Donald Trump") in December 2020 as part of the omnibus [Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021](/wiki/Consolidated_Appropriations_Act,_2021 "Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2021").[[417]](#cite_note-417)

### Philanthropy

Bieber supports [Pencils of Promise](/wiki/Pencils_of_Promise "Pencils of Promise"),[[418]](#cite_note-418) a charity founded by Adam Braun, the younger brother of Bieber's manager.[[419]](#cite_note-419) The organization builds schools in developing countries, and Bieber became manager for the organization's campaign in [Guatemala](/wiki/Guatemala "Guatemala"). He serves as a celebrity spokesman for the organization[[420]](#cite_note-pop-420) by running ads for the charity and its campaign "Schools4All".[[421]](#cite_note-421)[[422]](#cite_note-mtvschools-422) He promises to visit schools that donate the most funds to the organization.[[423]](#cite_note-423) He takes part in the charity's fund-raising galas and donates parts of the proceeds from his concerts and Someday line of fragrances,[[424]](#cite_note-someday-424) and various merchandising to the charity. In 2010, Bieber supported a campaign for [People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals](/wiki/People_for_the_Ethical_Treatment_of_Animals "People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals") (PETA) by urging fans to adopt abandoned pets from shelters.[[425]](#cite_note-425) Bieber donated his hair to [Ellen DeGeneres](/wiki/Ellen_DeGeneres "Ellen DeGeneres") during his appearance in her talkshow *[The Ellen DeGeneres Show](/wiki/The_Ellen_DeGeneres_Show "The Ellen DeGeneres Show")* in March 2011. His hair sold on [eBay](/wiki/EBay "EBay") for more than $40,000 and the proceeds benefited the animal rescue charity, The Gentle Barn.[[426]](#cite_note-426) Following the [earthquake and tsunami](/wiki/2011_T%C5%8Dhoku_earthquake_and_tsunami "2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami") in Japan in March 2011, Bieber donated proceeds from his concerts in Japan to [Japanese Red Cross](/wiki/Japanese_Red_Cross "Japanese Red Cross") in May 2011.[[427]](#cite_note-427) In December 2011, Bieber donated $100,000 to Whitney Elementary School in [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas") to provide students from low income families.[[428]](#cite_note-428) Bieber supported [Charity: Water](/wiki/Charity:_Water "Charity: Water") which is a nonprofit that brings potable drinking water to people in developing countries. On his birthday in 2011 and 2012, he launched his campaign to urge his followers to donate on Twitter.[[429]](#cite_note-429) Bieber was named top charitable celeb of 2011 by American news aggregator and blog [HuffPost](/wiki/HuffPost "HuffPost").

In 2013, Bieber launched his online #GiveBackPhilippines campaign for helping the victims of [Typhoon Haiyan](/wiki/Typhoon_Haiyan "Typhoon Haiyan") and travelled to the [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines") after raising $3 million. His work in the country earned him a star on the [Philippine Walk of Fame](/wiki/Eastwood_City_Walk_of_Fame "Eastwood City Walk of Fame").[[430]](#cite_note-430) He also supports Children's Miracle Network Hospitals and Alzheimer's Association.[[431]](#cite_note-431) In September 2017, Bieber donated $25,000 to the [American Red Cross](/wiki/American_Red_Cross "American Red Cross") to help people in [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas") after the severe destruction caused by [Hurricane Harvey](/wiki/Hurricane_Harvey "Hurricane Harvey").[[432]](#cite_note-432)

On February 7, 2020, Bieber donated $100,000 to Julie Coker, a 22-year-old fan who works in [mental health](/wiki/Mental_health "Mental health") awareness.[[433]](#cite_note-433) Coker revealed that her own past struggles with mental health motivated her to work for mental health. She praised Bieber by saying, "[Bieber] has a big following, so if he has a good message about mental health, hopefully everybody else . . . will want to start thinking about mental health in a different way."[[434]](#cite_note-434)

In February 2020, Bieber made a donation to Beijing Chunmiao Children Aid Foundation in China to support [COVID-19](/wiki/COVID-19 "COVID-19") relief.[[435]](#cite_note-435) Bieber and [Ariana Grande](/wiki/Ariana_Grande "Ariana Grande") collaborated on the single "[Stuck With U](/wiki/Stuck_with_U "Stuck with U")", released in May 2020 as the first of series of singles coordinated by Scooter Braun, who is also Bieber's manager, to support the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[436]](#cite_note-436) All net proceeds from the song went to the First Responders Children's Foundation to fund grants and scholarships for children of first responders and health care workers who worked on the front lines during the pandemic.[[437]](#cite_note-437) By August 2021, the single had raised over $3,500,000.[[438]](#cite_note-438) In September 2020, Bieber and [Chance the Rapper](/wiki/Chance_the_Rapper "Chance the Rapper") announced that they had partnered with [Cash App](/wiki/Cash_App "Cash App") and would donate $250,000 to fans who were struggling during the pandemic.[[439]](#cite_note-439)

In March 2021, Bieber visited the [California State Prison](/wiki/California_State_Prison,_Los_Angeles_County "California State Prison, Los Angeles County") in [Los Angeles County](/wiki/Los_Angeles_County,_California "Los Angeles County, California") along with his wife [Hailey](/wiki/Hailey_Baldwin "Hailey Baldwin") and pastor Judah Smith at the invitation of [Scott Budnick](/wiki/Scott_Budnick_(film_producer) "Scott Budnick (film producer)").[[440]](#cite_note-VisittoCAprison-440) Bieber met with inmates involved in The Urban Ministry Institute and expressed support for Budnick's [Anti-Recidivism Coalition](/wiki/Anti-Recidivism_Coalition "Anti-Recidivism Coalition"). During the visit, Bieber committed to provide buses to transport relatives of the inmates who have been unable to see them due to the [COVID-19 pandemic in California](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_California "COVID-19 pandemic in California").[[440]](#cite_note-VisittoCAprison-440) Bieber described his visit to the prison as a "life-changing experience that I will never forget".[[441]](#cite_note-ABCradio-441)

### Legal issues and controversies

Bieber had several run-ins with the law around the world before his first arrest in 2014,[[442]](#cite_note-APImage-442) including when he was accused of reckless driving in his neighbourhood in 2012, charged in Brazil with [vandalism](/wiki/Vandalism "Vandalism") in 2013,[[442]](#cite_note-APImage-442)[[443]](#cite_note-BBCFirstArrest-443) and was ordered to appear in [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina "Argentina") within 60 days by a [Buenos Aires](/wiki/Buenos_Aires "Buenos Aires") court to give testimony on an alleged assault on a photographer on November 9, 2013.[[444]](#cite_note-444) In April 2013, Bieber was criticized for writing a message in the guestbook at the [Anne Frank House](/wiki/Anne_Frank_House "Anne Frank House") that read, "Truly inspiring to be able to come here. Anne was a great girl. Hopefully she would have been a [belieber](/wiki/List_of_fandom_names "List of fandom names")."[[445]](#cite_note-445) After the message was posted on the museum's [Facebook](/wiki/Facebook "Facebook") page, Bieber received widespread criticism on social media for perceived insensitivity and narcissism. The Anne Frank House defended Bieber, stating, "He's 19. It's a crazy life he's living, he didn't mean bad ... He was very interested in the story of Anne Frank and stayed for over an hour. We hope that his visit will inspire his fans to learn more about her life and hopefully read the [diary](/wiki/The_Diary_of_a_Young_Girl "The Diary of a Young Girl")."[[446]](#cite_note-446)[[447]](#cite_note-447)

On January 23, 2014, Bieber was arrested in [Miami Beach, Florida](/wiki/Miami_Beach,_Florida "Miami Beach, Florida"), together with singer [Khalil](/wiki/Khalil_(singer) "Khalil (singer)"), on suspicion of [driving under the influence](/wiki/Driving_under_the_influence "Driving under the influence") (DUI), driving with an over-six-month-expired licence, and [resisting arrest](/wiki/Resisting_arrest "Resisting arrest") without violence.[[443]](#cite_note-BBCFirstArrest-443)[[448]](#cite_note-448)[[449]](#cite_note-449) Police said that Bieber told authorities he had consumed alcohol, smoked marijuana, and taken prescription drugs.[[442]](#cite_note-APImage-442) He was released from these charges on a $2,500 [bond](/wiki/Bail "Bail").[[450]](#cite_note-450) A toxicology report revealed that Bieber had [THC](/wiki/THC "THC") (a principal constituent of [cannabis](/wiki/Cannabis_(drug) "Cannabis (drug)")) and the [anti-anxiety medication](/wiki/Anxiolytic "Anxiolytic") [Xanax](/wiki/Alprazolam "Alprazolam") in his system at the time of his arrest.[[451]](#cite_note-451)[[452]](#cite_note-452) In January 2021, he reflected on this event, describing it as "not [his] finest hour" and encouraging fans to "let the forgiveness of Jesus take over and watch your life blossom into all that God has designed you to be."[[453]](#cite_note-453) On August 13, 2014, the January DUI case was settled with a [plea bargain](/wiki/Plea_bargain "Plea bargain"); Bieber pleaded guilty to resisting an officer without violence and a lesser charge of [driving without due care and attention](/wiki/Driving_without_due_care_and_attention "Driving without due care and attention"). He was fined US$500 and sentenced to attend a 12-hour anger management course and a program that teaches the impact of drunken driving on victims. As part of the plea bargain, he made a US$50,000 contribution to *Our Kids*, a local children's charity.[[454]](#cite_note-454)[[455]](#cite_note-455)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7c/Biebermugshot.jpg/250px-Biebermugshot.jpg)](/wiki/File:Biebermugshot.jpg)

Bieber's mugshots taken on January 23, 2014, after being detained by police in [Miami Beach](/wiki/Miami_Beach,_Florida "Miami Beach, Florida")

Following Bieber's arrest on the DUI charge, more than 270,000 people petitioned the [White House](/wiki/Executive_Office_of_the_President_of_the_United_States "Executive Office of the President of the United States") seeking to have him deported from the United States. Although the number of signatures received was sufficient to require a response under published [White House guidelines](/wiki/We_the_People_(petitioning_system) "We the People (petitioning system)"), the Obama administration declined substantive comment on the petition.[[456]](#cite_note-ENTmania.com-456) Immigration Law expert [Harlan York](/wiki/Harlan_York "Harlan York") noted that the likelihood of Bieber being deported was extremely slim. York stated, "About a decade ago, the Supreme Court ruled that driving under the influence, typically, is not a basis to deport someone."[[457]](#cite_note-457)

In June 2014, a video emerged of a 15-year-old Bieber telling a joke about black people, which used the word "[nigger](/wiki/Nigger "Nigger")" multiple times.[[458]](#cite_note-458) In the same month, a second video showed a 15-year-old Bieber giggling as he sings his song "[One Less Lonely Girl](/wiki/One_Less_Lonely_Girl "One Less Lonely Girl")", but parodying the main lyric as "One less lonely nigger", and stating that if he were to kill one, he would be "part of the [KKK](/wiki/Ku_Klux_Klan "Ku Klux Klan")".[[459]](#cite_note-459)[[460]](#cite_note-460) He apologized the day the latter was released: "Facing my mistakes from years ago has been one of the hardest things I've ever dealt with."[[461]](#cite_note-461)

One of his neighbours in [Calabasas, California](/wiki/Calabasas,_California "Calabasas, California"), accused Bieber of throwing eggs at his home on January 9, 2014, and causing thousands of dollars of damage.[[442]](#cite_note-APImage-442)[[462]](#cite_note-462)[[463]](#cite_note-BBCegg-463) On July 9, 2014, Bieber was charged with one [misdemeanour](/wiki/Misdemeanor "Misdemeanor") count of vandalism in California for throwing eggs at his Calabasas neighbour's home in January.[[463]](#cite_note-BBCegg-463)[[464]](#cite_note-LATegg-464)[[465]](#cite_note-ReutersEgg-465) Police earlier claimed that they had video footage of him high-fiving friends after the eggs were thrown.[[463]](#cite_note-BBCegg-463)[[466]](#cite_note-466) He pled [no contest](/wiki/Nolo_contendere "Nolo contendere") to the charge. Since then, he has permanently moved to [Beverly Hills, California](/wiki/Beverly_Hills,_California "Beverly Hills, California").[[463]](#cite_note-BBCegg-463)[[464]](#cite_note-LATegg-464)[[465]](#cite_note-ReutersEgg-465) On September 1, 2014, Bieber was arrested and charged with assault and dangerous driving near his hometown of [Stratford, Ontario](/wiki/Stratford,_Ontario "Stratford, Ontario"), after a collision between a minivan and Bieber's all-terrain vehicle on August 29. Ontario police said that he then "engaged in a physical altercation" with an occupant of the minivan. He was released shortly and his lawyer blamed the incident on "the unwelcome presence of [paparazzi](/wiki/Paparazzi "Paparazzi")".[[467]](#cite_note-467)[[468]](#cite_note-BBC:_Quad_bike-468)

> I didn't want to come off arrogant or conceited, or basically how I've been acting the past year, year and a half ... although what's happened in the past has happened, I just want to ... be kind and loving and gentle and soft.

—Bieber reflects in a video posted in January 2015[[469]](#cite_note-469)

In July 2017, the Chinese government banned Bieber from performing in China. A Chinese Bieber fan contacted the [Beijing Municipal Bureau of Culture](/wiki/Beijing_Municipal_Bureau_of_Culture "Beijing Municipal Bureau of Culture") requesting the reason for the ban. The Bureau released a statement, explaining "Justin Bieber is a gifted singer, but he is also a controversial young foreign singer", and "In order to maintain order in the Chinese market and purify the Chinese performance environment, it is not suitable to bring in badly behaved entertainers."[[470]](#cite_note-470)[[471]](#cite_note-471) In 2021, Chinese streaming sites [Youku](/wiki/Youku "Youku"), [iQIYI](/wiki/IQIYI "IQIYI"), and [Tencent Video](/wiki/Tencent_Video "Tencent Video") removed Bieber's scenes in *[Friends: The Reunion](/wiki/Friends:_The_Reunion "Friends: The Reunion")* donning [Ross Geller](/wiki/Ross_Geller "Ross Geller")'s "Spudnik" costume.[[472]](#cite_note-472) However, his albums *Changes* (2020) and *Justice* (2021) have been officially released in China, with Bieber seen promoting both albums on Chinese music platforms.[[473]](#cite_note-473)[[474]](#cite_note-474)
